title: CITRUS AND SHRIMP LEMON LAVENDER CUPS

ingredients.name: medium shrimp (peeled, deveined, and cut into bite-sized pieces)
dried culinary lavender (finely chopped or ground)
oranges (such as navel or Cara Cara, segmented)
crisp endive (white, red, or a combination; leaves separated)

steps: Season and sear shrimp: Combine the bite-sized shrimp pieces, chopped dried lavender, and ½ tsp of kosher salt in a medium bowl, tossing well to coat. Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.

Add the seasoned shrimp in a flat layer and cook undisturbed for 2 minutes. Flip the pieces over and cook for another 2 minutes until they turn pink and completely opaque.Assemble the citrus base: Whisk the fresh lemon juice, lemon zest, orange zest, and ⅛ tsp of kosher salt together in a separate large bowl.

Gently fold in the orange segments, diced Persian cucumber, and chopped fresh mint until the dressing evenly coats the fruit and vegetables.Toss together: Dump the hot, pan-seared lavender shrimp directly into the bowl with the cool citrus and cucumber mixture. Toss everything together thoroughly so the warmth of the shrimp coaxes out the aromatic oils of the fresh mint and citrus zests.Spoon and serve: Arrange the separated crisp endive leaves across a large serving platter to act as your "cups".

Spoon a generous portion of the citrus shrimp mixture into each leaf cavity, ensuring every cup gets a couple of pieces of shrimp. Drizzle any remaining pan juices from the bowl over the top and serve immediately.

title: BRAISED SHORT RIBS WITH RED WINE SAUCE (GF/DF)

ingredients.name: large bone-in beef short ribs (approx. 2 1/2 to 3 lbs total)
dry bold red wine (such as Cabernet Sauvignon or Merlot)

steps: Preheat and sear: Preheat your oven to 325°F (160°C). Pat the beef short ribs completely dry with paper towels and season all sides generously with salt and black pepper. Heat the vegetable oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Sear the short ribs for 2 to 3 minutes per side until a deep, dark brown crust forms. Remove the ribs to a plate.

Sauté the aromatics: Turn the heat down to medium. Add the diced onion, carrots, and celery to the remaining fat in the pot. Cook for 5 to 6 minutes until the vegetables soften. Stir in the minced garlic and tomato paste, cooking constantly for 2 minutes until the paste darkens and smells sweet.

Deglaze and simmer: Pour the red wine into the pot, using a wooden spoon to vigorously scrape up all the highly flavorful browned bits stuck to the bottom. Bring to a boil and let the wine simmer for 5 minutes until reduced by half. Pour in the beef broth and add the rosemary, thyme, and bay leaves.

Bake and reduce sauce: Nestled the seared short ribs and their juices back into the liquid. Cover tightly with a lid and transfer to the preheated oven. Braise for 2½ to 3 hours until the meat is completely fork-tender and falling off the bone. Remove the ribs to a warm platter. Strain the braising liquid into a saucepan, skim off the top layer of fat, and simmer on high for 10 minutes until it thickens into a glossy, rich red wine glaze. Pour the sauce over the ribs and serve hot.

title: GARLIC GREEN BEANS
steps: Blanch beans: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook green beans for 3 minutes, then immediately drain and shock in ice water to lock color.
Sauté garlic: Melt butter and olive oil together in a large skillet over medium heat. Add thinly sliced garlic and sauté for 1 minute until fragrant.
Flash sauté: Add the drained green beans into the hot skillet, tossing continuously for 2 to 3 minutes until coated and heated through.
Brighten flavor: Turn off heat, season with kosher salt and black pepper, drizzle with fresh lemon juice, and serve immediately.

title: HONEY GLAZED CARROTS

ingredients.name: carrots (peeled and sliced diagonally into 1/4-inch coins)

steps: Simmer carrots: Combine butter, honey, vegetable broth, and sliced carrot coins in a medium skillet over medium heat.
Steam tender: Cover skillet with a lid and cook for 5 to 6 minutes until the carrot coins begin to soften when pierced.
Reduce glaze: Remove lid and increase heat to medium-high. Cook uncovered for 4 to 5 minutes, stirring frequently, until liquid reduces to a glossy syrup.
Season and garnish: Toss with kosher salt and black pepper, remove from heat, garnish with chopped fresh parsley, and serve hot.

title: ROASTED BABY POTATOES

ingredients.name: small baby red or yellow potatoes (halved)

steps: Preheat and prep: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Wash and halve baby potatoes, ensuring they are completely dry before seasoning.
Toss potatoes: Place potatoes in a large bowl, drizzle with olive oil, and toss with minced garlic, dried thyme, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Arrange on pan: Spread the baby potatoes out cut-side down in a single flat layer on a large rimmed baking sheet.
Roast till crisp: Roast for 25 to 30 minutes, flipping them halfway through, until the outsides are golden-crisp and insides are fork-tender.

title: SAUTEED SHRIMP WITH GARLIC, WHITE WINE & FRESH DICED TOMATO
steps: Sear shrimp: Heat olive oil and butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Season shrimp with salt and pepper; sear 2 minutes on first side.
Add aromatics: Flip shrimp, add minced garlic and diced tomatoes immediately. Sauté 1 to 2 minutes until garlic is fragrant.
Deglaze pan: Pour in white wine and lemon juice, scraping up pan bits. Simmer 2 minutes until shrimp are opaque, toss with parsley, and serve hot.

title: GRILLED ASPARAGUS

ingredients.name: fresh asparagus spears (tough woody ends snapped off)

steps: Preheat grill: Preheat your outdoor grill or indoor grill pan to medium-high heat (approx. 400°F / 200°C).
Toss asparagus: Place trimmed asparagus spears on a plate, drizzle with olive oil, and toss with minced garlic, salt, and black pepper until fully coated.
Grill spears: Place asparagus spears directly across grill grates perpendicular to bars. Grill for 5 to 6 minutes, rolling them occasionally, until tender and lightly charred.
Brighten and plate: Remove from grill, transfer to a platter, drizzle immediately with fresh lemon juice, garnish with shaved Parmesan, and serve.

title: BUTTERED PEAS

ingredients.name: frozen sweet peas (no need to thaw)

steps: Heat liquid: Combine butter, water, salt, garlic powder, and black pepper in a medium saucepan over medium heat; stir until butter melts and simmers.
Cook peas: Add frozen sweet peas directly into the simmering liquid. Cook uncovered for 3 to 4 minutes, stirring frequently, until the peas are plump, hot, and vibrant green.
Evaporate moisture: Continue cooking for 1 extra minute until most of the liquid pan moisture evaporates into a light glaze coating the peas.
Garnish: Stir in chopped fresh chives, remove from heat, transfer to a serving dish, and serve hot.

title: WILD RICE PILAF

ingredients.name: wild rice blend (rinsed thoroughly in a fine-mesh strainer)

steps: Sauté onion: Melt butter in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Sauté diced onion for 4 to 5 minutes until soft and translucent.
Toast grains: Add rinsed wild rice blend to the pan, stirring constantly for 2 minutes to toast the grains and coat them in butter.

Simmer covered: Pour in chicken or vegetable broth, salt, and black pepper. Bring to a rolling boil, then immediately reduce heat to low, cover tightly with a lid, and simmer undisturbed for 40 minutes.
Steam and fluff: Remove saucepan from heat, keep lid on, and let steam for 5 minutes. Remove lid, fluff with a fork, stir in fresh parsley, and serve hot.

title: CHOCOLATE MOUSSE WITH SPONGE TOFFEE

ingredients.name: large eggs (separated into yolks and whites)

steps: Melt chocolate: Melt chopped chocolate and butter together in a heatproof bowl set over a pot of simmering water (double boiler), stirring until completely smooth; remove and let cool slightly.
Whisk bases: Whisk the egg yolks into the melted chocolate one at a time. In a separate clean bowl, whip egg whites and sugar until stiff peaks form. In a third bowl, whip heavy cream to soft peaks.
Fold together: Gently fold the whipped cream into the chocolate mixture, then carefully fold in the whipped egg whites in batches until a uniform, airy mousse forms.
Chill and serve: Spoon mousse into dessert cups and refrigerate for 2 hours to set. Just before serving, top generously with the crumbled crunchy sponge toffee.

title: HERB ROASTED CHICKEN LEG WITH LEMON SAUCE (GF/DF)

ingredients.name: skin-on chicken legs (thigh and drumstick attached)

steps: Preheat and roast: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Rub chicken legs with olive oil and coat with poultry seasoning, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and pepper. Roast on a wire rack over a baking sheet for 35 to 40 minutes until skin is deeply crispy and internal temperature reads 175°F (79°C).
Simmer base: Melt dairy-free butter in a small saucepan over medium heat. Pour in chicken broth and lemon juice; bring to a simmer.
Thicken sauce: Whisk the cornstarch slurry into the simmering liquid. Cook constantly for 2 minutes until the sauce thickens smoothly without dairy flour.
Plate and garnish: Plate the crispy roasted chicken legs, spoon the bright lemon sauce around or over the meat, garnish with fresh parsley, and serve hot.

title: BAKED COD WITH LEMON CAPER VINAIGRETTE (GF/DF)

ingredients.name: wild-caught cod fillets (approx. 6 oz each, thick cuts preferred)

steps: Preheat oven: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C) and line a baking dish with parchment paper.
Season fish: Pat cod fillets completely dry with paper towels. Rub with 1 tbsp olive oil and season both sides evenly with garlic powder, salt, and black pepper. Place fillets in the baking dish.
Bake fillets: Bake for 10 to 12 minutes, depending on thickness, until the fish flakes easily with a fork and turns opaque.

Make vinaigrette and serve: Whisk extra-virgin olive oil, lemon juice, lemon zest, capers, minced shallot, and parsley together in a small bowl. Plate the warm fish and spoon the vinaigrette generously over top.

title: GRILLED SUMMER ZUCCHINI

ingredients.name: medium summer zucchinis (ends trimmed, cut lengthwise into 1/2-inch spears)

steps: Preheat grill: Preheat outdoor grill or indoor griddle pan to medium-high heat (approx. 400°F / 200°C).
Season spears: Place zucchini spears in a shallow dish, drizzle with olive oil, and toss with minced garlic, Italian seasoning, salt, and black pepper.
Grill cut-side down: Place seasoned zucchini spears cut-side down directly onto the oiled grates. Grill undisturbed for 3 minutes to set grill marks.
Flip and finish: Flip spears over and grill for another 2 to 3 minutes until tender-crisp. Remove to a platter, drizzle with fresh lemon juice, and serve hot.

title: ROASTED BUTTERNUT

ingredients.name: large butternut squash (approx. 2 to 3 lbs, peeled, seeded, and cut into 1-inch cubes)

steps: Preheat and prep: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Peel the butternut squash, split lengthwise, scoop out seeds, and chop into uniform 1-inch cubes.
Coat squash cubes: Place squash cubes on a large rimmed baking sheet, drizzle with olive oil and maple syrup, then sprinkle with thyme, salt, and black pepper.
Toss and arrange: Toss everything directly on the pan to coat evenly, then spread into a single flat layer ensuring cubes do not overlap.
Roast caramelized: Roast for 25 to 30 minutes, flipping them halfway through, until fork-tender and beautifully caramelized at the edges; serve hot.

title: QUINOA PILAF

ingredients.name: uncooked quinoa (rinsed thoroughly in a fine-mesh strainer)

steps: Sauté aromatics: Heat butter or olive oil in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Sauté diced onion for 4 minutes until translucent.
Toast quinoa: Add the rinsed and drained quinoa to the pan. Stir constantly for 2 minutes to cook off excess moisture and lightly toast the grains.
Simmer covered: Pour in broth, salt, and black pepper; bring to a boil. Immediately cover tightly with a lid, turn heat to low, and simmer for 15 minutes.
Steam and serve: Remove pan from heat, keep lid closed, and let steam for 5 minutes. Remove lid, fluff with a fork, stir in chopped fresh chives, and serve hot.

title: CREAM OF CARROT SOUP
steps: Sauté base: Melt butter in a medium pot over medium heat. Sauté diced yellow onion and minced garlic for 4 to 5 minutes until soft and translucent.

Simmer carrots: Add chopped carrots, broth, salt, black pepper, and ground ginger to the pot. Bring to a rolling boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes until carrots are completely tender.
Purée silky: Remove pot from heat. Use an immersion blender directly in the soup pot to purée the liquid until completely smooth and velvety.
Finish and garnish: Stir in the heavy cream and warm through on low heat for 1 minute. Ladle into bowls and garnish with fresh chopped chives.

title: SEARED SCALLOPS WITH CITRUS & HERB SALSA   (GF/DF)

ingredients.name: large sea scallops (tough side muscles removed)

steps: Chop citrus salsa: Dice the grapefruit and orange segments into small pieces. Toss with diced red onion, chopped cilantro, chopped mint, olive oil, and a pinch of salt in a bowl; set aside.
Dry scallops: Pat sea scallops completely dry on both sides with paper towels; season surfaces evenly with salt and white pepper.
Sear hot: Heat vegetable oil and dairy-free butter in a large skillet over high heat until smoking hot. Add scallops in a single layer, ensuring they do not touch.
Flip and plate: Sear undisturbed for 2 minutes until a deep golden crust forms. Flip and sear for 1 more minute. Transfer to plates immediately and spoon the vibrant citrus herb salsa over top.

title: ROASTED LAMB LEG WITH MINT GREMOLATA (GF/DF)

ingredients.name: boneless leg of lamb (rolled and tied)

steps: Preheat and rub: Preheat your oven to 325°F (165°C). Rub the tied lamb leg with olive oil, minced garlic, chopped rosemary, salt, and black pepper, pressing the rub deep into the meat.
Roast meat: Place lamb on a rack in a roasting pan. Roast for 1 hour and 15 to 30 minutes until a meat thermometer inserted into the thickest part reads 135°F (57°C) for medium-rare.
Chop gremolata: While lamb roasts, combine finely chopped mint, parsley, grated garlic, lemon zest, and 2 tbsp extra-virgin olive oil in a small bowl; mix well.
Rest and carve: Transfer lamb to a cutting board, tent with foil, and rest for 15 minutes. Remove twine, slice thinly, and top with the fresh mint gremolata.

title: SAUTEED SPINACH WITH DICED ONIONS

ingredients.name: fresh baby spinach leaves (washed and thoroughly dried)

steps: Sauté onion base: Heat olive oil in a large skillet or wok over medium heat. Sauté finely diced yellow onion for 3 to 4 minutes until soft and translucent.
Flash cook garlic: Add minced garlic to the skillet and cook constantly for 30 seconds until fragrant, ensuring it does not brown.
Wilt spinach leaves: Turn heat up to medium-high and add fresh baby spinach in large handfuls. Toss continuously with tongs for 2 minutes until leaves are just wilted.
Season and serve: Remove skillet from heat immediately to prevent wateriness. Season with kosher salt and black pepper, toss one final time, and serve warm.

title: ROASTED CARROTS WITH CARDAMON SEASONING

ingredients.name: carrots (peeled and sliced diagonally into 1-inch thick chunks)

steps: Preheat oven: Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C) and line a large baking sheet with parchment paper.
Season carrot chunks: Place carrot pieces into a large bowl. Drizzle with olive oil and honey, then sprinkle with ground cardamom, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper; toss thoroughly.
Spread on pan: Spread the seasoned carrots across the prepared baking sheet in a single flat layer, ensuring pieces do not overlap.
Roast caramelized: Roast for 20 to 25 minutes, tossing halfway through, until carrots are fork-tender and beautifully caramelized at the edges.

title: WHALDORF ENDIVE SALAD
steps: Whisk dressing: Combine mayonnaise or Greek yogurt, fresh lemon juice, salt, and black pepper in a medium bowl; mix until smooth.
Toss fillings: Add diced apple, halved grapes, diced celery, and chopped walnuts to the dressing bowl. Fold gently until everything is fully coated.
Prep cups: Cut off the bottom core of the endive heads and separate the firm, scoop-shaped leaves.
Stuff and serve: Arrange endive leaves across a platter. Spoon a generous amount of the Waldorf mixture into each leaf cavity and serve chilled.

title: BEEF STRIPS WITH SAUTEED WILD MUSHROOMS WITH PAN SAUCE (GF)

ingredients.name: sirloin or flank steak (sliced across grain into 1/2-inch strips)
mixed wild mushrooms (shiitake, cremini, oyster, sliced)

steps: Sear beef: Pat meat dry, season with garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Heat olive oil in a skillet over high heat. Sear beef strips for 2 minutes until browned; remove to a plate.
Sauté mushrooms: Lower skillet heat to medium, melt 1 tbsp butter, and sauté wild mushrooms and chopped shallot for 5 to 6 minutes until mushrooms are browned.
Build gravy: Add minced garlic and cook 30 seconds. Pour beef broth into pan, scraping bits. Simmer 3 minutes until broth reduces slightly.
Glaze meat: Stir in the remaining 1 tbsp cold butter and fresh parsley to gloss the sauce. Return beef strips to the skillet, toss for 1 minute to coat, and serve hot.

title: BAKED HADDOCK WITH TOMATO&BASIL SALSA (GF/DF)
steps: Toss salsa: Combine quartered cherry tomatoes, red onion, fresh basil, extra-virgin olive oil, red wine vinegar, and salt in a bowl; set aside at room temp.
Prep haddock: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C) and line a pan with parchment paper. Pat haddock fillets dry, rub with olive oil, and season with garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Bake fillets: Bake for 10 to 12 minutes until the fish turns opaque and flakes apart easily with a fork.
Serve: Place baked fillets onto plates and spoon the fresh, juicy tomato-basil salsa generously across the top.

title: STEAMED BROCCOLI

ingredients.name: large head of broccoli (cut into uniform bite-sized florets)
a squeeze of fresh lemon juice

steps: Prep florets: Cut the broccoli head into uniform florets so they cook evenly; rinse thoroughly under cold water.
Boil water: Pour water into a pot with a steamer basket, ensuring water doesn't touch the basket. Bring to a rolling boil over high heat.
Steam green: Put broccoli into the basket, cover with a tight lid, and steam over medium-high heat for 4 to 5 minutes until tender-crisp.
Season: Toss immediately in a serving bowl with butter or olive oil, salt, and a squeeze of fresh lemon juice; serve hot.

title: FRESH HERB ORZO
steps: Boil orzo: Bring salted water to a rolling boil. Cook orzo for 8 to 9 minutes until al dente; drain fully.
Heat binder: Return the empty pot to low heat, melt butter or warm olive oil, and add the broth, salt, and pepper.
Toss pasta: Add the cooked orzo back into the pot, stirring constantly for 1 minute until the broth creates a light coating.
Fold herbs: Stir in the fresh chopped parsley, dill, and chives just before removing from heat; serve warm.

title: ASSORTED DESSERTS

ingredients.name: A curated assortment of pre-made mini tarts
and fresh seasonal berries for display

steps: Arrange sweets: Group pastries neatly by type on a large slate or ceramic serving platter.
Garnish platter: Scatter fresh raspberries and blueberries into empty spaces between desserts for color.
Serve: Present at room temperature or chilled as a shareable dessert board.

title: ASIAN VEGETABLE - BEEF SOUP
steps: Sauté aromatics: Heat vegetable oil in a large soup pot over medium heat. Sauté grated ginger and minced garlic for 1 minute until fragrant.
Simmer broth base: Pour beef broth, soy sauce, fish sauce, and the bruised lemongrass stalk into the pot; bring to a simmer for 10 minutes.
Cook meat and veg: Discard lemongrass. Add mushroom slices, shredded carrots, and beef strips. Simmer for 5 minutes until meat is cooked through.
Wilt greens: Drop baby bok choy into the pot and simmer for 2 more minutes until leaves are wilted. Ladle into deep bowls and top with green onions.

title: ROASTED PORK WITH GRAINY DIJON MUSTARD SAUCE (GF/DF)

ingredients.name: large pork tenderloin (approx. 1 to 1.5 lbs, trimmed)
canned full-fat coconut milk or cashew cream

steps: Season tenderloin: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Rub pork with olive oil, thyme, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Sear in a hot skillet for 2 minutes per side.
Roast pork: Move skillet into the oven. Roast tenderloin for 15 to 20 minutes until internal temperature hits 145°F (63°C). Rest on a cutting board tented with foil.
Simmer mustard sauce: Melt dairy-free butter in a pan over medium heat. Sauté shallot for 2 minutes, pour in chicken broth and both mustards, whisking to combine.
Glaze sauce: Simmer sauce for 3 minutes to reduce, then stir in coconut milk or cashew cream until smooth. Slice pork into medallions and drape with sauce.

title: HONEY GARLIC CHICKEN BREAST

ingredients.name: boneless skinless chicken breasts (pounded to even thickness)

steps: Dust chicken: Season dry chicken breasts with salt and pepper. Lightly dredge in flour, shaking off all excess coating.
Sear golden: Heat olive oil and butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Cook chicken for 5 to 6 minutes on the first side until golden.
Simmer glaze sauce: Flip chicken, turn heat down to medium, and scatter minced garlic into pan. Pour honey, soy sauce, and vinegar around the chicken.
Coating thick: Simmer sauce uncovered for 4 to 5 minutes, spooning it continuously over the chicken, until the glaze is thick and chicken is cooked to 165°F (74°C).

title: GRILLED SHIRMP WITH MANGO SALSA
steps: Toss salsa: Combine diced mango, red bell pepper, red onion, fresh cilantro, and lime juice in a medium bowl; mix well and chill.
Season shrimp: Pat shrimp dry, toss with olive oil, garlic powder, chili powder, and salt in a bowl until evenly coated.
Grill skewered: Thread shrimp onto soaked wooden skewers. Grill over medium-high heat for 2 to 3 minutes per side until pink and opaque.
Plate and top: Slide grilled shrimp off skewers onto serving plates and top generously with large spoonfuls of the cold mango salsa.

title: ROASTED BRUSSEL SPROUTS
steps: Preheat and prep: Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C). Trim the dry stem ends off the Brussels sprouts and pull away any loose, yellow outer leaves.

Slice each sprout strictly in half lengthwise from the top down through the core so they stay intact.Coat and season: Place the halved Brussels sprouts directly into a large mixing bowl. Drizzle with the extra virgin olive oil, then sprinkle with the garlic powder, kosher salt, and freshly cracked black pepper.

Toss thoroughly with your hands or tongs until every sprout is uniformly slick with oil and spices.Arrange and roast: Transfer the sprouts onto a large rimmed baking sheet. Arrange them all cut-side down in a flat, single layer.

This direct contact with the hot pan ensures they get a deep, crispy brown sear. Roast in the preheated oven for 20 to 25 minutes until the outer leaves are beautifully charred and the centers are completely tender when pierced with a fork.Finish and serve: Remove from the oven.
Transfer the hot sprouts to a serving bowl. Drizzle with a light zig-zag of balsamic glaze if using for a sweet-and-tangy contrast, toss once gently, and serve hot.

title: BAKED POTATO WITH SOUR CREAM&CHIVE
steps: Preheat and pierce: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Pierce the clean Russet potatoes 5 times with a fork to let steam escape during baking.
Crisp skins: Rub the potato skins evenly with olive oil and coat with kosher salt. Place directly on the center oven rack.
Bake fully: Bake for 50 to 60 minutes until the skins are completely crisp and a knife slides effortlessly into the centers.

Open and load: Slice a long slit lengthwise down the center of each hot potato. Squeeze the ends to pop open, fluff the interior meat with a fork, and load with 1 tbsp butter, a dollop of sour cream, and fresh chives.

title: TACO SOUP

ingredients.name: (10 oz) diced tomatoes with green chilis (like Ro-Tel)
(15 oz) pinto or kidney beans (rinsed and drained)
low-sodium beef or vegetable brothFinish & Toppings: Tortilla chips

steps: Brown the meat: Heat the olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the ground beef and diced yellow onion.

Cook for 5 to 6 minutes, breaking the meat apart with a wooden spoon, until the beef is fully browned and the onions are soft. Drain off any excess grease from the pot.Infuse the seasonings: Turn the heat down to medium.

Stir in the minced garlic, taco seasoning packet, ground cumin, and kosher salt. Sauté constantly for 1 minute until the spices are highly fragrant and completely coat the meat mixture.Combine the cans: Pour in the diced tomatoes, diced tomatoes with green chilis, rinsed black beans, rinsed pinto beans, and drained corn.

Stir thoroughly to distribute the ingredients evenly.Simmer the soup: Pour in the broth and bring the mixture to a rolling boil. Once boiling, reduce the heat to medium-low, cover the pot tightly with a lid, and let simmer gently for 15 minutes to allow all the southwestern flavors to marry.
Remove the lid, ladle hot into deep bowls, and pile high with crushed tortilla chips, shredded Cheddar, and a dollop of sour cream.

title: WEDGE SALAD SKEWER

ingredients.name: sturdy wooden or bamboo appetizer skewers (about 6 inches long)
crumbled blue cheese (plus extra for garnish)

steps: Crisp the bacon: Cook the thick-cut bacon slices in a large skillet over medium heat for 8 to 10 minutes, turning occasionally, until completely crispy. Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate to drain and cool.

Once cooled, cut each bacon strip crosswise into 2-inch wide pieces (you will need 12 sturdy square pieces total).Cut the lettuce wedges: Rinse the head of iceberg lettuce under cold water and pat it completely dry. Cut the head in half through the core, then cut each half into smaller sections.

Slice the crisp leaves into 12 uniform, bite-sized square chunks (about 1½ to 2 inches wide), ensuring each chunk has multiple layered leaves that hold tightly together.Whisk the dressing: Combine the mayonnaise, sour cream, crumbled blue cheese, fresh lemon juice, grated garlic, kosher salt, and black pepper in a small serving bowl. Whisk vigorously, pressing down on some of the blue cheese chunks with your fork to build a rich, thick, and creamy consistency.

Cover and chill.Thread and assemble: Build each skewer by sliding on one cherry tomato, followed by one folded or flat 2-inch piece of crispy bacon, and finish by pushing the skewer securely through the center of a layered iceberg lettuce square chunk. Arrange the 12 skewers flat on a serving platter.

Drizzle the chilled blue cheese dressing heavily across the lettuce portions of the skewers, then garnish with extra crumbled blue cheese, chopped fresh chives, and a turn of black pepper. Serve immediately.

title: GREEN BEAN ALMONDINE
steps: Blanch beans: Boil green beans in salted water for 3 minutes until crisp-tender; drain and plunge into an ice bath, then dry.
Toast almonds: Melt butter in a large skillet over medium-low heat. Add almonds and cook 3 minutes, stirring constantly until golden and fragrant.
Sauté and serve: Toss in minced garlic for 30 seconds. Add the dry green beans, salt, and pepper. Toss for 2 minutes to heat through, splash with lemon juice, and serve.

title: ROASTED ROOT VEGETABLES (CARROT/YAM/PARSNIP/TURNIP)

ingredients.name: turnip (all peeled and cut into 1-inch chunks)

steps: Preheat and season: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Toss all root vegetable chunks with olive oil, minced garlic, thyme, salt, and pepper in a large bowl.
Spread on pan: Arrange chunks in a single flat layer across a large rimmed baking sheet, leaving gaps so they roast instead of steam.
Roast caramelized: Roast for 30 to 35 minutes, turning halfway through, until fork-tender and deeply browned at the edges.

title: BUTTER MASHED POTATOES

ingredients.name: Yukon Gold potatoes (peeled and cut into 1-inch chunks)

steps: Boil potato chunks: Place peeled potato chunks into a large pot. Cover with cold water by an inch and add a generous tablespoon of salt. Bring to a boil over high heat.
Cook tender: Cook for 12 to 15 minutes until the potatoes are completely tender when pierced with a fork; drain thoroughly in a colander.
Steam dry: Return drained potatoes immediately to the hot, empty pot over low heat for 1 minute, shaking gently to evaporate any leftover surface water.

Mash smooth: Remove from heat. Use a potato masher or ricer to break down potatoes completely. Pour in the melted butter and warm milk, seasoning with salt and pepper. Stir vigorously with a wooden spoon until smooth and velvety.

title: MANHATTAN CLAM CHOWDER (TOMATO BASE)

ingredients.name: (6.5 oz each) chopped clams (reserve the juice!)
clam juice (from cans plus bottled if needed)
medium Russet potatoes (peeled and diced into 1/2-inch cubes)

steps: Crisp bacon: Cook chopped bacon in a large pot over medium heat until crispy. Remove bacon with a slotted spoon and set aside, leaving the fat in the pot.
Sauté vegetables: Add diced onion, carrots, celery, and green bell pepper to the bacon fat. Sauté for 6 to 8 minutes until soft. Stir in minced garlic for 30 seconds.

Simmer potatoes: Pour in the diced tomatoes with their juices, the reserved clam juice, and broth. Add the diced potatoes, dried thyme, and bay leaves. Bring to a boil, then lower heat, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes until potatoes are tender.

Add clams and finish: Stir in the chopped clams from the cans and the reserved crispy bacon bits. Simmer uncovered for 5 minutes until the clams are heated through (do not overcook or they become rubbery). Discard bay leaves, season with salt and pepper, and serve hot.

title: BOSTON CREAM PIE

ingredients.name: (15 oz) pre-made vanilla pastry cream or pudding filling

steps: Bake sponge cake: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease and flour a 9-inch round cake pan. Whisk flour, baking powder, and salt. In a large bowl, beat eggs and sugar for 3 minutes until thick and pale. Fold in vanilla, melted butter, milk, and the dry ingredients. Pour into pan and bake 20 to 25 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean; cool completely.
Split cake: Carefully slice the cooled sponge cake horizontally in half using a long serrated knife to create two even round layers.

Layer filling: Place the bottom cake layer onto a serving plate. Spread the vanilla pastry cream smoothly over the top, then place the second cake layer gently over the cream filling; chill for 30 minutes.

Glaze with ganache: Heat heavy cream in a small saucepan until simmering; pour over chopped chocolate in a bowl and let sit for 2 minutes, then whisk until smooth. Pour the warm chocolate ganache over the top center of the cake, letting it drip elegantly down the sides. Chill for 1 hour before slicing.

title: TOMATO BASIL PIZETTE

ingredients.name: mini pre-made flatbreads or pita breads

steps: Preheat oven: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C) and place mini flatbreads on a large baking sheet.
Assemble layers: Brush flatbread surfaces lightly with olive oil. Spread 2 tablespoons of marinara sauce evenly across each.
Cheese and fruit: Top sauce with shredded mozzarella cheese and arrange thin tomato slices over the cheese layer.
Bake crisp: Bake for 8 to 10 minutes until the edges are crispy and cheese is bubbling; scatter fresh basil leaves over top and serve hot.

title: THAI CHICKEN NOODLE SOUP
steps: Sauté base: Heat vegetable oil in a large pot over medium heat. Sauté sliced onion and red curry paste for 2 minutes, stirring constantly to release aromas.
Simmer broth: Pour in chicken broth, coconut milk, fish sauce, and brown sugar. Bring to a boil, then turn heat down to medium-low and simmer uncovered for 5 minutes.
Cook noodles and veg: Stir in the dry rice noodles, sliced red bell pepper, and sliced mushrooms. Cook gently for 6 to 8 minutes until the noodles are soft and the vegetables are tender-crisp.

Add meat and finish: Fold in the shredded cooked chicken and cook for 2 minutes to heat through. Turn off heat, stir in fresh lime juice, ladle into deep bowls, and garnish generously with fresh cilantro and green onions.

title: CHICKEN CAESAR SALAD
steps: Grill chicken cutlets: Rub chicken cutlets with olive oil, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Sear in a hot skillet over medium-high heat for 4 minutes per side until cooked through to 165°F (74°C); slice into strips.
Whisk dressing: Combine mayonnaise, grated Parmesan, lemon juice, Dijon mustard, grated garlic, Worcestershire sauce, and black pepper in a small bowl; whisk vigorously until smooth.
Assemble base: Place chopped Romaine lettuce into a very large salad bowl. Pour dressing over lettuce and toss thoroughly until every leaf is coated.
Garnish and serve: Distribute the dressed lettuce among plates. Top with the warm chicken strips, garlic croutons, and heavily scatter shaved Parmesan cheese over top.

title: CARROT AND ORANGE SOUP
steps: Chop the onion and slice the carrots.
Zest and juice the orange.
If using fresh thyme, strip the leaves from the stems.
Toast the slivered almonds in a dry pan over medium heat until lightly golden and fragrant. Set aside.
In a medium pot, melt the butter or heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the onion and cook until soft, about 5 minutes.
Add the sliced carrots, vegetable broth, and thyme. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, until the carrots are very tender.
Remove the pot from the heat. Carefully blend the soup using an immersion blender until it's velvety smooth.
Stir in the orange zest and orange juice.
Season with salt and white pepper to taste. Gently reheat if necessary.

title: MAPLE GLAZED SALMON FILET WITH
steps: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Arrange salmon in 8-inch (20-cm) baking dish.
Microwave Becel® Buttery Taste margarine at HIGH until melted about 15 seconds. Stir in maple syrup, Dijon mustard and soy sauce. Pour over salmon.
Bake until salmon flakes with a fork, about 12 minutes. Serve, if desired, with hot rice and vegetables.

title: CHICKEN CORDON BLEU
steps: Combine the flour and paprika in a shallow pan.
Pound chicken breasts to 1/4 inch thick, then place one slice of Swiss and one slice of ham on each breast, leaving the edges clear.
Roll the chicken around the filling, coat each roll in the flour mixture, and secure with toothpicks.
Melt butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat and brown the chicken rolls on all sides, 5 to 8 minutes.
Add wine and bouillon, reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer until the chicken reaches 165 F, about 30 minutes.
Transfer chicken to a warm platter and remove toothpicks.
Whisk cream with cornstarch, add it slowly to the skillet juices, and cook 3 to 5 minutes until thickened.
Spoon the sauce over the chicken.

title: STEAMED CAULIFLOWER FLORETS

ingredients.name: washed and cut into bite-sized florets
melted butter (or extra virgin olive oil)

steps: Prep the cauliflower: Trim the outer green leaves from the cauliflower head and cut away the thick central core. Break or slice the remaining cauliflower into uniform, bite-sized florets so they cook at the exact same rate.Set up the steamer: Pour the water into a large pot and place a steamer basket inside, ensuring the water level sits just below the bottom of the basket.

Bring the water to a rolling boil over high heat.Steam the florets: Add the cauliflower florets into the steamer basket in an even layer. Cover the pot tightly with a lid, reduce the heat to medium-high, and steam for 4 to 5 minutes until the florets are tender when pierced with a fork but still hold their shape.Season and serve: Carefully remove the steamer basket from the pot to stop the cooking process.
Transfer the hot cauliflower to a serving bowl, drizzle evenly with the melted butter and lemon juice, toss gently with salt and pepper, and serve immediately.

title: STEAMED CARROTS

ingredients.name: peeled and sliced diagonally into 1/4-inch thick rounds

steps: Prep the carrots: Peel the carrots and trim off the top and bottom ends. Cut them on a slight diagonal into uniform ¼-inch thick coins so that they cook evenly.Set up the steamer: Pour the water into a medium pot and place a steamer basket inside, making sure the water does not touch the bottom of the basket.

Bring the water to a rolling boil over high heat.Steam the carrots: Add the sliced carrots into the steamer basket and spread them out. Cover the pot tightly with a lid, reduce the heat to medium-high, and steam for 5 to 6 minutes until the carrots are tender when pierced with a fork but not mushy.Season and serve: Carefully lift the steamer basket out of the pot.
Transfer the hot carrots to a serving bowl. Toss immediately with the butter, honey, and salt until the butter melts into a light glaze.
Garnish with fresh parsley and serve hot.

title: BLACK FOREST CAKE
steps: Preheat oven to 350 F; grease and flour two 9-inch round cake pans, line bottoms with parchment, and chill a mixing bowl for cream.
Whisk flour, sugar, cocoa, baking powder, baking soda, and salt in a large bowl.
Add eggs, milk, oil, and vanilla and beat until combined.
Divide batter between prepared pans and bake about 35 minutes, until a tester comes out clean.
Cool cake layers 10 minutes in pans, loosen edges, invert, and cool fully for 1 to 2 hours.
Drain cherries, reserving 1/2 cup juice; combine reserved juice, cherries, sugar, and cornstarch in a saucepan.
Cook cherry mixture over low heat, stirring, until thickened; stir in vanilla and chill completely.
Whip cream with confectioners sugar in the chilled bowl to stiff peaks.
Split each cake layer horizontally; crumble one layer and reserve crumbs.
Reserve 1 1/2 cups frosting for piping, then layer cake rounds with frosting and cherry topping.
Frost the sides, press reserved cake crumbs onto the sides, pipe the reserved frosting around the top and bottom edges, and spoon remaining cherry topping over the top.

title: ROASTED RED PEPPER HUMMUS AND PITA
steps: Preheat oven to 450°F (skip this part if using jarred roasted red peppers and jalapeno.

Roast the peppers: Place the red bell pepper strips and jalapeno in a small baking dish or cast-iron skillet. Drizzle generously with olive oil. Bake in a heated oven for 15 to 20 minutes or until tender and well-charred (turn peppers over halfway through). Remove from oven and let cool. Drain from oil.

Blend. In the bowl of a fitted with an S-blade, add the roasted bell pepper strips from one bell pepper and jalapeno along with the chickpeas, garlic, tahini, sumac, smoked paprika, and lemon juice. Drizzle a little extra virgin olive oil. Run the processor until you reach the desired creamy paste consistency. Test and adjust seasoning, if needed. Run the processor again to combine.

Serve: Transfer to a serving dish, and spoon swoops and swirls into the hummus. Mince the remaining pepper strips and sprinkle them over the hummus. Drizzle with extra virgin olive oil, and sprinkle a pinch of paprika or sumac for garnish, if you like. Serve with warm pita and sliced veggies.

title: SPICY TUNA AND CRISPY RICE CRACKER
steps: Cook sushi rice according to package directions. While warm, mix in the rice vinegar and sugar.
Press the seasoned rice firmly into a small rectangular pan or baking dish, about 1/2 inch thick. Chill in the refrigerator for at least 1 hour, or until firm.

While the rice chills, prepare the tuna. In a medium bowl, combine the finely chopped tuna, Japanese mayonnaise, sriracha, sesame oil, and sliced scallions. Mix gently until just combined. Refrigerate until ready to use.
Once the rice is firm, cut it into small rectangles (about 1x2 inches).
In a large skillet, heat about an inch of vegetable oil over medium-high heat.
Carefully fry the rice patties in batches for 2-3 minutes per side, until golden brown and crispy. Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.

title: SEARED HALIBUT WITH CHERRY TOMATO&OLIVE
steps: Pat halibut fillets dry and season with salt and pepper.
Sear halibut in a hot oiled skillet until browned and just cooked through.
Serve with tortilla chips for dipping.

title: PASTA WITH SUMMER VEGETABLES WITH LEMON PARMESAN SAUCE

ingredients.name: The Pasta & Vegetables8 oz short pasta (like penne, bowties, or rotini)1 tbsp olive oil1 medium zucchini
sliced into half-moons1 medium yellow squash
sliced into half-moons1 cup cherry tomatoes
halved1 cup fresh or frozen peas3 cloves garlic

mincedThe Lemon Parmesan Sauce2 tbsp unsalted butter1 large lemon (both zested and juiced) 1/2 cup freshly grated Parmesan cheese (plus more for serving) 1/2 cup reserved pasta water 1/4 cup fresh basil leaves
chopped or tornSalt and black pepper to tasteOptional: A pinch of crushed red pepper flakes

steps: Cook the Pasta: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook your pasta according to the package directions until it is just al dente.Save the Liquid Gold: Before draining the pasta, carefully scoop out and reserve at least ½ cup of the starchy pasta water.

Drain the pasta and set it aside.Sauté the Summer Veggies: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the zucchini and yellow squash, sautéing for 3 to 4 minutes until they begin to soften and slightly brown.

Stir in the minced garlic, peas, and cherry tomatoes, cooking for another 1 to 2 minutes until the garlic is fragrant and the tomatoes just begin to soften. Season lightly with salt and pepper, then transfer the veggies to a plate.Build the Emulsified Sauce: Turn the skillet heat down to low.

Melt the butter in the pan, then stir in the lemon juice, lemon zest, and half of your reserved pasta water. Whisk in the grated Parmesan cheese until it melts smoothly into the liquid.Toss and Finish: Add the cooked pasta and sautéed vegetables back into the skillet.

Toss everything together vigorously over low heat so the cheese, lemon, and pasta starch merge into a glossy sauce that clings to the noodles. If the pasta looks too dry, splash in a little more of your reserved pasta water.Garnish and Serve: Turn off the heat.
Stir in the fresh basil and optional red pepper flakes. Divide among bowls and finish with an extra sprinkle of Parmesan cheese and a crack of fresh black pepper.
This recipe makes 4 servings

title: ROASTED MUSHROOMS

ingredients.name: (approx. 680 g) mixed wild mushrooms (such as cremini, shiitake, and button)

steps: Preheat and prep: Preheat your oven to 425°F (220°C). Wipe the mushrooms clean with a damp paper towel to remove any dirt.

Trim the stem ends and cut large mushrooms into halves or quarters, leaving smaller ones whole so they roast evenly.Toss with coating: Place the mushrooms directly onto a large, rimmed baking sheet. Drizzle with the olive oil, melted butter, minced garlic, fresh thyme, salt, and black pepper.

Toss thoroughly with your hands or tongs to ensure every mushroom is evenly coated.Roast until deep gold: Spread the mushrooms out into a single, flat layer, ensuring they are not crowded together so they roast instead of steam. Roast in the preheated oven for 18 to 20 minutes, flipping them halfway through, until the mushrooms release their moisture, shrink slightly, and turn deeply golden and crisp at the edges.Finish and garnish: Remove the baking sheet from the oven.
Toss the hot mushrooms immediately with the balsamic vinegar if using, then transfer to a serving dish. Garnish generously with the fresh chopped parsley and serve hot.

title: SHAVED FENNEL AND ORANGE SALAD
steps: Use a sharp knife or mandoline to shave the fennel very thinly.
Segment the oranges over a bowl, reserving the juices.
Whisk reserved orange juice with olive oil, vinegar, salt, and pepper to make the dressing.
Toss shaved fennel with orange segments and dressing.
Finish with fennel fronds before serving.

title: CUCUMBER AND DILL SALAD
steps: Gather all ingredients.
Place cucumber slices in a colander. Generously sprinkle salt over cucumber; let sit until water starts to bead on the slices, about 10 minutes. Drain water from cucumber slices.
Whisk sour cream, vinegar, yogurt, mayonnaise, lime juice, sugar, and dill together in a large bowl until dressing is smooth.
Mix cucumber, onion, and celery into dressing until evenly coated; season with salt and pepper.

title: Chicken Thigh Marsala in Mushroom Sauce (GF/DF)
steps: To slice chicken scaloppine: Arrange 3 boneless, skinless chicken breasts halves sideways and slice them in half. With the knife blade parallel to the cutting surface, slice them in half again, making 4 scaloppine slices per chicken breast.

Generously sprinkle both sides of the chicken with salt and pepper. Spread ½ cup all-purpose flour on a large plate. Dredge the chicken in the flour to coat both sides, shaking off any excess. Transfer the floured chicken to a piece of parchment or wax paper as you go.

Place a large (preferably 12-inch) skillet over medium-high heat. Add one tablespoon each of the butter and olive oil. When the butter is melted and stops foaming, add 1 pound thick-sliced brown or white button mushrooms to the pan.

Cook in one layer without stirring until the mushrooms are golden brown on one side. Stir in ½ cup chopped shallot, 1-2 cloves finely chopped garlic, 2 teaspoons chopped fresh thyme and ¾ teaspoon salt. Cook 2-3 more minutes, stirring frequently, until the shallots are translucent. Transfer the mushrooms to a bowl.

Return the skillet to medium-high heat and add the remaining tablespoon of butter and olive oil. When the butter has melted, add the dredged chicken pieces to the pan. Cook about 5 minutes per side, until golden brown on both sides (they'll still be a undercooked at this point). Note: If you're pan isn't large enough to hold all the chicken without crowding, cook them in 2 batches.

Pour ½ cup dry Marsala wine into the pan — it should begin to boil instantly. Cook until the wine is almost completely evaporated and reduced to a sticky glaze. Add ½ cup heavy cream, ¼ cup chicken broth and the cooked mushrooms (including all the juice they exuded), nudging the chicken here and there while gently stirring the sauce. Bring to a simmer and cook until the sauce is lightly thickened, and the chicken is cooked through, 5-7 minutes
Remove the pan from the heat. Sprinkle the parsley over the chicken and serve.

title: Open Faced Roast Beef Dip on French Baguette with Side Jus (DF)
steps: Prepare the steak. Generously season the steak with salt and pepper. Heat a large cast-iron skillet over high heat and sear the steak until it reaches medium-rare, depending on thickness about 3 minutes per side, or until it reaches 135°F on an instant-read thermometer. Let rest for 5 minutes. Slice the steak into strips, as thin as possible against the grain and set aside.

Make the au jus. In a medium saucepan over medium heat, whisk the beef stock, thyme, salt, sherry, Worcestershire, garlic powder, and onion powder, until fully combined. Simmer, stirring occasionally for 5 minutes.
Butter the inside of each roll, and sprinkle with the 2 teaspoons garlic powder. Place open-faced on a rimmed baking sheet.
Turn the oven broiler to HIGH. Quickly dip the strips of steak into the au jus and place in even amounts onto one side of each roll. Sprinkle ½ cup of the cheese on each sandwich.
Place the sheet pan under the broiler until the cheese begins to melt, watching carefully, about 2 minutes. Remove from the oven and sprinkle with parsley, if desired.
Ladle ¾ cup of au jus into small ramekins. Serve with the warm sandwiches for dipping.

title: LEMON BAKED COD
steps: Preheat oven to 400 degrees F.
Mix together the lemon juice, olive oil, and melted butter in a shallow bowl (do not add the garlic yet). Set aside.
In another shallow bowl, mix the all-purpose flour, spices, salt and pepper. Set next to the lemon sauce.
Pat the fish dry. Dip the fish in the lemon sauce then dip it in the flour mixture. Shake off excess flour. Reserve the lemon sauce for later.

Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a cast iron skillet (or an oven-safe pan) over medium-high heat (watch the oil to be sure it is shimmering but not smoking). Add the fish and sear on each side to give it some color, but do not fully cook (about 2 minutes on each side). Remove the skillet from heat.
To the remaining lemon sauce, add the minced garlic and mix. Drizzle all over the fish fillets.
Bake in the heated oven until the fish flakes easily with a fork (10 minutes should do it, but begin checking earlier). Remove from the heat and sprinkle chopped parsley. Serve immediately.

title: ROASTED BABY TOP CARROTS

ingredients.name: bunches true baby carrots (tops still attached)
a squeeze of fresh lemon juice

steps: Prep and trim: Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C). Slice off the green leafy tops of the baby carrots, leaving about 1 inch of the green stems intact for an elegant, rustic presentation.

Scrub the skins well under cold water but do not peel them, then pat completely dry with a towel.Toss with glaze: Whisk together the olive oil, melted butter, honey, minced garlic, salt, and black pepper in a small bowl. Place the prepped baby carrots on a large rimmed baking sheet lined with parchment paper or foil, then pour the glaze mixture over them and toss until evenly coated.Arrange and roast: Spread the carrots out into a single flat layer on the baking sheet so they roast instead of steaming.

Roast in the preheated oven for 20 to 25 minutes, flipping them halfway through, until they are fork-tender and beautifully caramelized around the edges.Garnish and serve: Transfer the hot roasted carrots to a serving platter. Drizzle with a final squeeze of fresh lemon juice to brighten the flavor, and sprinkle with chopped fresh herbs before serving hot.

title: GARLIC MASHED POTATOES

ingredients.name: potatoes, preferably Yukon Gold or another yellow, waxy potato

steps: Preheat oven to 400 F.
Remove loose papery skin from the garlic head, slice off the tops of the cloves, drizzle with olive oil, salt lightly, wrap in foil, and roast 30 to 40 minutes until soft and beginning to brown.
Peel potatoes, cut into 1-inch chunks, place in a saucepan with 1/2 teaspoon salt, and cover with cold water.
Bring potatoes to a boil, reduce to a simmer, and cook about 15 minutes until fork-tender.
Warm cream and butter together until the butter melts.
Drain potatoes, return them to the pot over low heat, squeeze in the roasted garlic, and mash.
Add warm cream and butter and mash to the desired texture without overmixing.

title: BANANA CREAM PIE
steps: Make the pie crust through step 5 according to my directions and video tutorial in my pie crust recipe. Make pie dough in advance because it needs to chill in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours before rolling out (step 2). The recipe makes 2 crusts, and you only need 1 for this pie, so save the other for another pie.

On a floured work surface, roll out one of the discs of chilled dough. Turn the dough about a quarter turn after every few rolls until you have a circle 12 inches in diameter. Carefully place the dough into a 9-inch pie dish. Tuck it in with your fingers, making sure it is completely smooth. To make a lovely thick edge, I do not trim excess dough around the edges. Instead, fold the excess dough back over the edge and use your hands to mold the edge into a rim around the pie. Crimp the edges with a fork or use your fingers to flute the edges. (Review this how to crimp and flute pie crust page if you need extra help with this step.) If dough is too warm, fluting/crimping will be difficult, so you can always chill it in the refrigerator for 5–10 minutes first. After fluting/crimping the edges, chill the pie dough in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes and up to 5 days. Cover the pie crust with plastic wrap if chilling for longer than 30 minutes. Chilling the shaped dough helps prevent the crust from shrinking.
While the crust is chilling, preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).

Line the chilled pie crust with parchment paper. (Crunch up the parchment paper first so that you can easily shape it into the crust.) Fill with pie weights or dried beans. (Note that you will need at least 2 standard sets of pie weights to fit.) I like to push the weights up against the sides of the pie crust to help ensure the sides don’t shrink down. Bake until the edges of the crust are starting to brown, about 15–16 minutes. Remove pie from the oven and carefully lift the parchment paper (with the weights) out of the pie. Prick holes all over the bottom crust with a fork. If desired for a shiny golden crust, brush egg wash on edges. Return pie crust to the oven and bake for 14–15 minutes, or until bottom has browned. Remove from the oven, and cool on a wire rack completely before filling. You can blind bake the crust up to 3 days ahead of time. Cover cooled crust tightly and refrigerate until ready to fill. (Review this how to par-bake pie crust page if you need extra help with this blind-baking step.)

Whisk the egg yolks and cornstarch together. (A fork works nicely for this, or a small whisk.) Set aside. Combine the milk, heavy cream, sugar, and salt in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Whisk until all the sugar has dissolved, then bring to a gentle simmer, whisking occasionally. Once simmering, remove about 1/2 cup (120ml) of the milk mixture and, in a slow and steady stream, whisk into the egg yolk and cornstarch mixture. Keep those egg yolks moving so they don’t scramble. In a slow and steady stream, pour and whisk the egg yolk mixture into the pot. The pudding will immediately begin to bubble and thicken. Stand back and use caution as the bubbles may burst. Cook for 1 full minute, whisking constantly.

Remove the pan from heat and whisk in the vanilla and butter. You will have about 3 cups of filling. If you notice a lot of little chunks of egg yolk, they may not have been tempered properly. To fix, you can run the warm custard through a fine mesh sieve.

Place a piece of plastic wrap directly on top of the warm pudding to prevent a skin from forming on top. Allow to cool for 15 minutes. I do not recommend any longer than this, as it will be too thick to spread neatly into the crust.

Slice 2–3 bananas and arrange slices into the cooled pie shell. Sprinkle with cinnamon, if desired. Spread custard on top (it’s ok if it is still slightly warm). An offset spatula is helpful for spreading. Place a piece of plastic wrap directly on top of the custard; again, to prevent a skin from forming. Refrigerate for at least 4 hours and up to 1 day.

Using a handheld or stand mixer fitted with a whisk attachment, whip the heavy cream, sugar, and vanilla extract on medium-high speed until medium peaks form, about 3–4 minutes. Medium peaks are between soft/loose peaks and stiff peaks and are the perfect consistency for topping and piping on desserts.

Pipe or spread the whipped cream on top. (I use Wilton 1M tip.) Slice remaining banana and garnish with banana slices and serve pie immediately, or refrigerate for up to 1 hour and then garnish with banana slices and serve. (Best to garnish with banana slices immediately before serving, but whipped cream can go on the pie up to 1 hour in advance.)
Slice and serve. Cover leftovers and store in the refrigerator for up to 5 days.

title: CORN CHOWDER

ingredients.name: salt and ground black pepper to taste

steps: Cook bacon in a large pot over medium-high heat until crisp, 5 to 7 minutes; drain, leaving bacon and 2 tablespoons bacon fat in the pot.
Add potatoes and onion to the pot and cook about 5 minutes.
Pour in water, bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer until potatoes are tender, about 20 minutes.
Stir in cream-style corn and half-and-half and heat through without boiling.
Season with salt and black pepper.

title: Mango and Avocado Salad.  Choice Dressing (GF/DF)
steps: Whisk olive oil, lime juice, honey, cumin, chili powder, salt, and pepper until emulsified.
Add chopped mangoes, chopped avocados, diced red onion, and chopped cilantro to the bowl.
Toss gently to coat the fruit and vegetables with the dressing without crushing the avocado.

title: CHICKEN PARMESAN
steps: Gather the ingredients. Preheat the oven to 450 degrees F (230 degrees C).

Place chicken breasts between two sheets of heavy plastic (resealable freezer bags work well) on a solid, level surface. Firmly pound chicken with the smooth side of a meat mallet to a thickness of 1/2-inch.
Season chicken thoroughly with salt and pepper. Using a sifter or strainer, sprinkle flour over chicken breasts, evenly coating both sides.

Beat eggs in a shallow bowl and set aside. Mix bread crumbs and 1/2 cup Parmesan cheese in a separate bowl, set aside. Dip a flour-coated chicken breast in beaten eggs. Transfer breast to the bread crumb mixture, pressing crumbs into both sides. Repeat for each breast. Let chicken rest for 10 to 15 minutes.

Heat 1/2 inch of olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until it begins to shimmer. Cook chicken in batches, so you do not overcrowd the pan, until golden, about 2 minutes per side. The chicken will finish cooking in the oven.

Transfer chicken to a baking dish. Top each breast with 2 tablespoons tomato sauce. Layer each chicken breast with equal amounts of mozzarella cheese, fresh basil, and provolone cheese. Sprinkle remaining Parmesan over top and drizzle each with 1/2 teaspoon olive oil.

Bake in the preheated oven until cheese is browned and bubbly and chicken breasts are no longer pink in the center, 15 to 20 minutes. An instant-read thermometer inserted into the center should read at least 165 degrees F (74 degrees C). Let chicken rest briefly before serving.

title: ROASTED ZUCCHINI CHUNKS

ingredients.name: a squeeze of fresh lemon juice

steps: Preheat and prep: Preheat your oven to 425°F (220°C). Cut each trimmed zucchini lengthwise into quarters, then slice crosswise into thick 1-inch chunks so they maintain their texture without getting mushy.Toss with oil and spices: Place the zucchini chunks directly onto a large rimmed baking sheet.

Drizzle with the olive oil, then add the minced garlic, dried Italian seasoning, salt, and black pepper. Toss thoroughly until every chunk is uniformly coated.Arrange and roast: Spread the zucchini chunks out into a single flat layer, ensuring they do not crowd or overlap so they roast and brown instead of steaming.

Roast in the preheated oven for 15 to 20 minutes, flipping them halfway through, until the edges are beautifully caramelized and the center is tender.Finish and garnish: Remove the baking sheet from the oven. Toss the hot zucchini immediately with a light squeeze of fresh lemon juice, then top with the grated Parmesan cheese if using.
Serve hot or at room temperature.

title: ANCIENT GRAINS

ingredients.name: blend (e.g., farro, quinoa, spelt, or barley)

steps: Rinse the grains: Place the grain blend in a fine-mesh strainer. Rinse thoroughly under cold water to remove bitter saponins or excess starch.Sauté aromatics: Heat olive oil in a medium saucepan over medium heat.

Sauté the minced shallot and garlic for 2 minutes until soft and fragrant.Toast the grains: Add the rinsed grains to the pan. Stir continuously for 2 minutes to toast them lightly, enhancing their natural nutty flavor.Simmer: Pour in the broth and bring the mixture to a boil.

Reduce heat to low, cover with a tight-fitting lid, and simmer for 20 to 25 minutes until the liquid is fully absorbed.Steam: Remove the saucepan from the heat. Keep the lid on and let the grains steam undisturbed for 5 minutes.Finish and serve: Fluff the grains with a fork.
Fold in the fresh parsley, chives, lemon juice, lemon zest, salt, and pepper. Serve warm.

title: SMOKED SALMON CUCUMBER BITES
steps: Form a cup in each cucumber slice by using a melon baller to scoop a 1/2-inch-deep depression from one side. Place cucumbers, scooped-sides down, onto paper towels to drain for 15 minutes.
Mix cream cheese, chopped dill, lemon zest, lemon juice, and pepper in a bowl until well combined.
Spoon about 1/2 teaspoon cheese mixture into each cucumber cup. Top each cup with a salmon strip and a dill sprig.

title: GAZPACHO SOUP

ingredients.name: red bell peppers, roasted, peeled, and seeded
English cucumber, peeled and roughly chopped
cold water (or more, for desired consistency)
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
Garnish: Diced avocado, chopped cilantro, a drizzle of olive oil

steps: Use jarred roasted red peppers or roast your own and allow them to cool completely.
Peel and chop the cucumber and red onion. Peel the garlic clove.
Combine the roasted red peppers, chopped cucumber, red onion, garlic, olive oil, sherry vinegar, and cumin in a high-speed blender.
Blend on high until the mixture is completely smooth and silky.
With the blender running on low, slowly stream in the cold water until you reach your desired consistency.
Transfer the gazpacho to a container and chill in the refrigerator for at least 1 hour, or until thoroughly cold.

title: SPAGHETTI AND MEATBALLS IN MARINARA
steps: In a Dutch oven or large pan, heat olive oil over medium heat.
Add onion and saute for a few minutes until softened and fragrant.
Add garlic and saute another minute.
In a medium saucepan, combine all sauce ingredients and mix well.
CHEF'S TIP: For chunky sauce, add all sauce ingredients to the onion and garlic.
If you prefer smooth sauce, place all ingredients PLUS the garlic and onion mixture into a blender and process until smooth.
Return mixture to the pan.Bring to a gentle boil over medium-high heat.
Reduce heat and simmer for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Season to taste with garlic salt.
In a large bowl using a fork, combine all of the meatball ingredients.
Gently mix with your fork so as not to smash the ingredients together.
Too much working of the mixture can cause your meatballs to turn out tough.
Once everything is mixed, use your hands to be sure everything is combined well.
Work with hands for no longer than one minute.
Just long enough for everything to come together.
Use a cookie scoop to create meatballs.
I don’t roll mine, just place them straight from the cookie scoop into the pan.
You can roll them in your palms first, but again, only for a moment, don’t overwork the meat!
Heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil over medium heat in large saute pan or skillet.
Brown meatballs on both sides, creating a nice crust.
Remove meatballs and drain fat.
Don’t scrape out any bits of meat from the pan!CHEF'S TIP: This makes a lot of meatballs.
I usually only cook about 10-12 in the marinara, and freeze the rest of the browned meatballs.
Deglaze the pan with 2 tablespoons of wine.
Return meatballs to the pan, pour marinara over the meatballs and bring to a gentle boil over medium-high heat.
Reduce heat and simmer, uncovered, for a minimum of 30 minutes.
You can simmer the meatballs for up to 2 hours if you like, but you may need to add a little water throughout the process to keep the marinara from thickening too much.
Serve over cooked spaghetti noodles.

title: GRILLED TILAPIA WITH BALSAMIC ONION SAUCE
steps: Prep the fish: Rub tilapia fillets with 1 tablespoon of olive oil. Season both sides evenly with garlic powder, paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Set aside.Caramelize onions: Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add sliced red onions and a pinch of salt.
Cook for 8 to 10 minutes until soft and deeply browned.Simmer the sauce: Pour the balsamic vinegar, broth, and honey into the skillet with the onions. Bring to a simmer.

Reduce heat to medium-low and cook for 5 minutes until the liquid reduces by half and thickens into a glaze. Stir in cold butter if using, then remove from heat.Grill the tilapia: Preheat your grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
Oil the grates well. Grill the tilapia fillets for 3 to 4 minutes per side.

The fish is done when it flakes easily with a fork and reaches an internal temperature of 145°F (63°C).Assemble: Plate the grilled tilapia directly next to or on top of your herbed ancient grains. Spoon the warm balsamic onion sauce generously over the fish.
Garnish with fresh parsley.

title: BROCCOLINI WITH GARLIC CONFIT

ingredients.name: bunches broccolini (ends trimmed, thick stalks split lengthwise)
pre-made garlic confit cloves plus 2 tbsp of the confit oil
fresh lemon juiceSeasoning: Flaky sea salt and red pepper flakes (to taste)??

steps: Prep the broccolini: Rinse the broccolini and shake off excess water. Cut any unusually thick stalks in half lengthwise so all pieces cook evenly.Heat the oil: Warm 2 tablespoons of the garlic confit oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.Sear the stalks: Add the broccolini to the pan in a single layer.

Let it cook undisturbed for 2 to 3 minutes until the underside starts to develop bright green and slightly charred spots.Steam tender: Pour 2 tablespoons of water into the skillet. Immediately cover with a tight lid to trap the steam.
Cook for 3 minutes until the stalks are tender-crisp.Incorporate garlic: Remove the lid and turn the heat down to low. Add the ¼ cup of garlic confit cloves.

Use a spoon to gently smash the soft garlic cloves into the oil, coating the broccolini.Finish: Toss everything together for 1 minute until heated through. Remove from heat, squeeze the fresh lemon juice over the top, and season with flaky sea salt and a pinch of red pepper flakes.

title: SUMAC SPICED ROASTED CARROT CHUNKS
steps: Preheat: Set your oven to 425°F (220°C) and line a large baking sheet with parchment paper.Prep the carrots: Cut the carrots into uniform 1-inch chunks so they roast evenly.Season: Place the carrot chunks in a large mixing bowl. Drizzle with olive oil and toss until well coated.

Add the sumac, garlic powder, cumin, salt, and pepper. Toss again to evenly distribute the spices.Roast: Spread the carrots across the baking sheet in a single layer, ensuring they do not crowd each other.

Roast for 20 to 25 minutes, tossing halfway through, until the edges are deeply caramelized and the centers are fork-tender.Serve: Transfer the warm roasted carrots to your serving plate and garnish with fresh mint or parsley if desired.

title: HERB AND CREAM CHEESE POLENTA
steps: Boil liquids: Combine vegetable broth and milk in a medium saucepan. Bring to a gentle boil over medium-high heat.Whisk in cornmeal: Reduce heat to low.

Slowly whisk in the cornmeal in a steady stream to prevent lumps.Simmer and stir: Cook for 20 to 25 minutes, stirring frequently with a wooden spoon. The polenta is ready when it is thick, smooth, and pulls away slightly from the sides of the pan.Meltdown: Remove from heat.
Stir in the cream cheese and butter until fully melted and completely incorporated.Fold in herbs: Add the chives, thyme, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Stir well to distribute the fresh herbs.
Serve immediately while hot and creamy.

title: Griilled Zucchini Roll with Herbed Ricotta (GF)
steps: Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
Trim zucchini ends and slice zucchini lengthwise into 1/4-inch-thick strips.
Brush zucchini slices lightly with olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
Grill slices in a single layer about 2 minutes per side until pliable with light grill marks, then cool on a baking sheet.
Combine ricotta, chopped basil, mint, 1/4 teaspoon salt, and freshly ground black pepper.
Spread a thin layer of ricotta mixture across each zucchini strip.
Place a whole basil leaf at one end of each strip, roll from that end, and repeat with remaining slices.

title: BACON AND CLAM FLATBREAD
steps: Preheat oven to 475 F.
Cover a baking sheet pan with parchment paper or foil and set aside.
Place butter in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Add garlic and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add flour and cook, whisking constantly for 1 minute.
Add milk slowly, whisking constantly to keep lumps from forming, and cook until mixture has thickened slightly.
Remove from heat and stir in Parmesan cheese.
Season with salt and pepper.
Spread sauce over naan.
Arrange clams over sauce.
Sprinkle the Mozzarella or Cheddar, herbs de Provence and bacon over the top.
Bake until cheese is bubbly, about 8 minutes.
Season with additional salt and pepper to taste.

title: WILD MUSHROOM TART WITH GOAT CHEESE
steps: Preheat the oven to 400F.
Mix goat cheese with honey, olive oil, thyme chopped leaves, salt, and pepper until creamy and spreadable. Set aside.

In a large skillet add the 1 tablespoon olive oil over medium-high heat. Add the shallots and mushrooms. Sauté until all liquid has evaporated and the mushrooms starting to turn golden at the edges, 5 minutes. Add the brandy and cook until evaporated, less than 1 minute. Season with salt and pepper to taste.

Defrost the puff pastry and roll it out on a floured surface until it is half the thickness that you started with. With a sharp knife cut 1 inch off from each side. With a small baking brush, brush the egg mixture at the outer edges of the pastry. Place the 1-inch strip of puff pastry over it to form a riser border. The outer edges must be aligned one on top of the other properly.

Place the puff pastry over a silpat or parchment paper in a cookie sheet. Spread the goat cheese inside the puff pastry and top with the mushrooms. Spread some of the egg wash over the puff pastry border.
Bake in the warm oven for 20 minutes. Let it rest for a couple of minutes and serve.

title: ROASTED MEXICAN STREET CORN
steps: Preheat the oven to 425℉.
Slice the butter until several pats and dot them around a sheet pan. Place the pan in the oven to melt the butter.
When the butter is starting to brown, add the corn and toss well to coat.
Place the sheet pan back into the oven and cook for 6-8 minutes.
Remove from the oven and drizzle with Mexican crema and sprinkle with chili powder and cotija cheese. Top with chopped cilantro if desired.

title: BRAISED BEAN CASSOULET

ingredients.name: (15 oz each) Cannellini or Great Northern beans (rinsed and drained)

steps: Sauté aromatics: Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large Dutch oven or heavy-bottomed pot over medium heat. Add the onion, carrots, and celery.

Cook for 8 minutes until soft.Build flavor: Stir in the minced garlic and tomato paste. Cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly, until the paste turns a deep brick-red color.Deglaze: Pour in the white wine, using a wooden spoon to scrape up any browned bits from the bottom of the pot.
Simmer for 3 minutes until the liquid reduces by half.Simmer: Add the rinsed beans, vegetable broth, diced tomatoes, thyme, rosemary, and the bay leaf. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low.

Cover and simmer for 25 minutes until the sauce thickens.Toast topping: While simmering, mix the panko breadcrumbs with 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a small bowl. Toast in a separate skillet over medium heat for 3 minutes until golden brown.Finish: Remove the herb sprigs and bay leaf from the cassoulet.
Season with salt and pepper to taste. Stir in the fresh parsley and top with the toasted breadcrumbs just before serving.

title: KEY LIME PIE
steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (177°C).

Using a food processor, pulse the graham crackers and macadamia nuts together into coarse crumbs. A few larger pieces of nuts is OK. Pour into a medium bowl and stir in the sugar. Add the melted butter and stir until combined. The mixture will be thick, coarse, and sandy. Try to smash/break up any large chunks. Pour the mixture into an ungreased 9-inch pie dish. Use your hand to pat down the crumbs into the bottom and up the sides to make a crust. Use medium pressure—simply pat down until the mixture is no longer crumbly. Tips: You can use a small flat-bottomed measuring cup to help press down the bottom crust and smooth out the surface, but do not pack down too hard. And run a spoon around the bottom “corner” where the edge and bottom meet to help make a rounded crust⁠—this helps prevent the crust from falling apart when you slice into the pie. For more shaping technique tips, see the graham cracker crust recipe page.
Pre-bake crust for 8 minutes. Remove from the oven and leave the oven on.
Whisk the sweetened condensed milk, lime juice, and egg yolks together (or use an electric mixer). Whisk in the lime zest. Pour into warm crust.

Bake the pie for 18–20 minutes or until only *slightly* jiggly in the center. You want it mostly set. Remove from the oven and allow to cool completely on a wire rack. Once cool, cover and chill for at least 1 hour (and up to 3 days) before serving.
Garnish as desired.
Store leftovers in the refrigerator for up to 1 week.

title: ZUCCHINI FRITTERS WITH DIP
steps: Grate the zucchini using either the grating attachment on a food processor or the large holes on a box grater.
Sprinkle the zucchini with 1 teaspoon of salt, gently mix, then spread out onto a large kitchen towel and let sit for about 20 minutes to release its liquid.
Fold up the towel and wring it our over a bowl or sink to extract the liquid. Be aggressive and really squeeze out as much liquid as your can.
In a medium bowl, mix together the chives or scallions, egg, flour, corn starch, baking powder, pepper and the remaining ½ teaspoon of salt.
Add the zucchini and mix until everything is thoroughly combined.
Heat about a ¼- ½ inch of oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat, then line a baking sheet with paper towels and set it aside nearby.

Carefully drop heaping spoonfuls of the zucchini mixture into the hot oil and lightly press with the back of the spoon to flatten. Be careful not to overcrowd the pan. About 4 per batch is usually just right.
Cook until the edges are golden, about 3 to 4 minutes. Flip the fritters and fry them on the other side for another 2-3 minutes, until nicely browned, then remove to the paper towels to drain.
To make the sauce, add the yogurt, garlic, lemon juice, salt, pepper, chives or scallions and herbs in a small bowl and mix to combine. Taste, and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the fritters while they're hot with the lemon herb yogurt on the side.
To serve later, place the fritters in a 200 ℉ oven until ready to serve.

title: SOBA NOODLE SALAD

ingredients.name: Juice of half a lime ((full lime if the lime is small or less juicy))
sesame oil (, plus more for coating noodles)
soy sauce ((use tamari for gluten-free, coconut aminos for soyfree))
asian chile sauce (sambal oelek) (or sriracha or other hot sauce to taste)
Soba noodles (, use pure buckwheat soba or rice noodles for gluten-free)
Sesame seeds (, green onion, cilantro/mint (optional), crushed roasted peanuts/cashews(optional) for garnish)
optional additions: edamame (, baked tofu, other crunchy veggies etc.)

steps: Cook the noodles according to instruction on the package. (Simmer in boiling water for 4 to 5 mins for the wheat + buckwheat soba). Drain and rinse with cold water.
Add the noodles to a bowl and add a tsp of oil or sesame oil and toss to coat.
Add the veggies and half of the green onions to the bowl.
Add the dressing to the bowl and toss well.
Garnish with green onions, crushed nuts(optional) and sesame seeds (optional). Chill and serve.

title: SHRIMP LINGUINI WITH SAFFRON SAUCE AND PEPPERED GREENS

ingredients.name: (450g) large shrimp (peeled, deveined, and tails removed)
freshly cracked black pepperSeasoning & Finish: Salt to taste

steps: Bloom the saffron: Crush the saffron threads slightly with your fingers into a small bowl. Pour the 3 tablespoons of warm broth over them.
Let sit for 10 minutes to draw out the deep golden color and aroma.Cook the pasta: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook the linguini according to package instructions until al dente.
Reserve ½ cup of pasta water, then drain the noodles.Sear the shrimp: Heat 1 tablespoon each of butter and olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Season shrimp lightly with salt.
Sear for 1 to 2 minutes per side until pink and opaque. Remove shrimp from the skillet and set aside.Build the sauce: Turn heat down to medium.

Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of butter to the same skillet. Sauté the minced shallot and garlic for 2 minutes until soft.Deglaze and reduce: Pour in the white wine, scraping up the browned bits from the bottom.
Simmer for 3 minutes until reduced by half. Stir in the heavy cream and the bloomed saffron liquid (including the threads).

Simmer gently for 4 minutes until the sauce turns bright yellow and thickens slightly.Toss and assemble: Add the cooked linguini and seared shrimp into the skillet. Toss continuously for 1 to 2 minutes until the pasta is coated in the golden sauce, adding a splash of reserved pasta water if the sauce gets too thick.

Stir in the Parmigiano-Reggiano.Dress the greens: In a quick bowl, toss the fresh arugula with the extra-virgin olive oil, lemon juice, and a heavy pinch of freshly cracked black pepper.Serve: Plate a swirl of the golden saffron linguini and shrimp. Top each portion with a loose handful of the peppered arugula just before serving.

title: ROASTED CHICKEN BREAST IN CREAMY SUNDRIED TOMATO SAUCE
steps: Breast: Cut chicken in half horiztonally to form 2 thin steaks.
Sprinkle chicken with salt and pepper. Heat oil from sun dried tomat jar in a large skillet over high heat.
Add the chicken and cook until golden brown on both sides - 6 minutes total for thigh or 4 minutes for breast. Remove onto a plate, keep warm.
Add the garlic and cook for 15 seconds until light golden.
Add wine. Bring to simmer and scrape the bottom of the skillet to mix all the brown bits into the wine.
Let the wine simmer for 1 minute or so until the smell of alcohol is gone which means it has evaporated.
Mix in mustard, then add the chicken broth, cream, parmesan and sun dried tomatoes.
Bring to simmer, then reduce heat to medium and simmer for 2 to 3 minutes until thickened. Stir regularly to dissolve parmesan cheese.
Taste sauce and add more salt and pepper if needed.
Stir through basil leaves, then return chicken into pan. Spoon sauce over and simmer 1 minute.
Serve immediately! Great served over mashed potato, Creamy Mashed Cauliflower or pasta. Or serve with bread on the side to mop up sauce!

title: SEARED SALMON WITH BASIL PESTO SAUCE
steps: Heat a dry skillet, over medium heat, and lightly toast pine nuts (gently toss and shake pan occasionally); then place in a food processor.
To the food processor, add basil and garlic; pulse just to combine.
With processor running, drizzle in olive oil, and process, scraping down sides once or twice with a rubber spatula, to desired consistency (I like it sort of chunky).
Add in Parmesan cheese, red pepper flakes, lemon juice, and salt and pepper to taste; pulse to combine; set aside.
Wipe the salmon dry with paper towels (to allow a nice sear), then season lightly with salt and pepper.
Heat the skillet, over medium-high heat, then spray with cooking spray.
Cook salmon about 4 minutes per side or to desired doneness (I like it a little pinkish in the center since it will continue cooking a little when removed from the pan).
Serve salmon with pesto spooned over top.
Can also be served at room temperature.

title: SAUTEED ASPARAGUS (2" CUTS)

ingredients.name: tough ends snapped off and stalks cut diagonally into 2-inch pieces

steps: Prep the asparagus: Hold each asparagus spear near the bottom end and bend it until it naturally snaps, discarding the woody base.
