title: 3-SISTERS SOUP

ingredients.name: medium butternut or acorn squash (peeled, seeded, and cut into 1-inch cubes)

steps: Sauté the base: Heat the sunflower oil or butter in a large soup pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the diced onion, chopped carrots, and chopped celery.

Sauté for 6 to 8 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables begin to soften and the onions become translucent.Infuse the aromatics: Stir the minced garlic, dried thyme, kosher salt, and black pepper into the vegetable base. Cook constantly for 1 minute until highly fragrant, being careful not to let the garlic brown.Simmer the squash: Pour the broth into the pot and add the cubed winter squash and the bay leaf.

Bring the liquid to a rolling boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to medium-low. Cover tightly with a lid and simmer gently for 10 to 12 minutes, or until the squash is just fork-tender.Add the remaining sisters: Remove the lid and stir in the beans and corn kernels.
Simmer uncovered for an additional 5 to 8 minutes until the beans and corn are heated through and the squash is completely tender. Remove the bay leaf, adjust the salt to taste, and serve hot.

title: PAN SEARED SALMON WITH MAPLE DIJON SAUCE (GF/DF)

ingredients.name: skin-on or skinless salmon fillets (approx. 6 oz each)

steps: Whisk glaze: Combine pure maple syrup, Dijon mustard, tamari, and minced garlic in a small bowl; stir until smooth and set aside.
Season fillets: Pat salmon fillets completely dry with paper towels. Rub the flesh side lightly with olive oil and season with garlic powder, salt, and black pepper.

Sear salmon: Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Place salmon fillets skin-side down (if skin-on) and cook undisturbed for 4 to 5 minutes until crisp. Flip carefully and cook the flesh side for 2 to 3 minutes.
Glaze pan: Turn the heat to low, pour the maple dijon glaze directly into the skillet, and spoon it over the salmon for 1 minute until bubbling and sticky; serve immediately.

title: Peach Melba with Vanilla Ice Cream and Toasted Almonds (GF)

ingredients.name: high-quality vanilla bean ice cream (Certified Gluten-Free)

steps: Poach the peaches: Combine the water, granulated sugar, vanilla extract, and 1 tbsp of lemon juice in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Stir until the sugar dissolves and bring to a gentle simmer.

Lower the peach halves into the liquid and poach for 5 to 7 minutes until tender when pierced with a fork. Carefully remove the peaches with a slotted spoon, slide off their skins, and place the halves in the refrigerator to chill.Make the raspberry sauce: While the peaches chill, place the fresh raspberries, powdered sugar, and 1 tsp of lemon juice into a blender or food processor.

Blend on high speed for 1 minute until completely smooth.Strain the seeds: Pour the puréed raspberry mixture through a fine-mesh sieve set over a bowl, using the back of a spoon to press the liquid through while trapping and discarding the seeds. Cover the smooth Melba sauce and refrigerate until cold.Assemble and serve: To serve, place a large scoop of vanilla bean ice cream into each of 4 dessert dishes or coupe glasses.

Nestled two chilled poached peach halves cut-side down next to or on top of the ice cream. Drizzle a generous amount of the vibrant raspberry Melba sauce over the peaches and ice cream, then garnish with the toasted flaked almonds and a fresh mint leaf.

title: GRILLED CHICKEN BREAST WITH BASIL PESTO/ROASTED TOMATO

ingredients.name: small chicken breasts (boneless, skinless, pounded to an even 1/2-inch thickness)

steps: Preheat grill: Preheat your outdoor grill or indoor grill pan to medium-high heat (approx. 400°F / 200°C) and brush grates with oil.
Season chicken: Rub chicken breasts with 1 tbsp olive oil, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper. Grill chicken for 5 to 6 minutes per side until internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C).
Blister tomatoes: While chicken cooks, toss halved cherry tomatoes with 1 tbsp olive oil, salt, and pepper in a small hot skillet over high heat for 3 minutes until blistered.
Assemble plate: Place grilled chicken breasts on serving plates, spread a generous layer of basil pesto over each, top with warm blistered tomatoes, and garnish with fresh basil leaves.

title: SAUTEED SPINACH WITH DICED ONIONS/GOLDEN RAISINS

ingredients.name: fresh baby spinach leaves (washed and thoroughly dried)

steps: Sauté onion base: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Sauté finely diced yellow onion and golden raisins for 3 to 4 minutes until onions are soft and raisins plump up.
Flash cook garlic: Add minced garlic to the skillet and cook constantly for 30 seconds until fragrant, ensuring it does not brown.
Wilt spinach leaves: Turn heat up to medium-high and add fresh baby spinach in large handfuls. Toss continuously with tongs for 2 minutes until leaves are just wilted.
Season and serve: Remove skillet from heat immediately to prevent wateriness. Season with kosher salt and black pepper, toss one final time, and serve warm.

title: NO-BAKE BLUEBERRY CHEESECAKE
steps: Press crust: Combine graham cracker crumbs, sugar, and melted butter in a bowl. Press firmly into the bottom of a 9-inch springform pan; freeze for 10 minutes.
Beat filling: Beat the softened cream cheese, powdered sugar, and vanilla extract together in a large bowl with an electric mixer until completely smooth and creamy.
Whip cream: In a separate cold bowl, whip heavy cream until stiff peaks form. Gently fold the whipped cream into the cream cheese base in batches until uniform.
Layer and chill: Pour filling smoothly over the crust. Spread blueberry pie filling evenly across the top layer. Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours before slicing.

title: ROASTED PARSNIPS WITH CINNAMON BUTTER

ingredients.name: parsnips (peeled, cored if large, cut into 3-inch batons)

steps: Preheat pan: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C) and line a large baking sheet with parchment paper.
Mix coating: Whisk melted butter, olive oil, cinnamon, brown sugar, and kosher salt together in a small bowl until smooth.
Season parsnips: Place parsnip batons in a large bowl, pour the cinnamon-butter glaze over them, and toss thoroughly to coat each piece.
Roast caramelized: Spread parsnips in a single flat layer on the sheet pan. Roast for 20 to 25 minutes, flipping halfway, until edges are tender and caramelized.

title: BANANA CAKE WITH CINNAMON WHIPPED TOPPING

ingredients.name: overripe bananas (approx. 3 to 4 medium bananas, mashed smooth)

steps: Preheat and whisk dry: Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease a 9x13-inch metal or glass baking pan and line the bottom with parchment paper.

In a medium mixing bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, baking powder, baking soda, and kosher salt until free of lumps.Cream and build wet mix: Beat the room-temperature butter and granulated sugar together in a large bowl with an electric hand or stand mixer for 3 minutes until light and fluffy. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition, then blend in the vanilla extract, smooth mashed overripe bananas, and sour cream until uniform.Combine and bake: Turn the mixer to low speed.

Add the dry flour mixture to the wet base in two batches, mixing strictly just until the flour disappears—do not overmix, or the banana cake will lose its fluffy texture. Spread the thick batter smoothly into the prepared pan.

Bake for 28 to 32 minutes until the top is golden-brown and a toothpick inserted into the very center comes out clean. Remove from the oven and let cool completely in the pan on a wire rack.Whip and frost: Pour the cold heavy whipping cream, powdered sugar, ground cinnamon, and vanilla extract into a chilled glass or metal bowl.

Whip with an electric mixer on medium-high speed for 3 to 4 minutes until stiff, pillowy peaks form. Spread the cinnamon whipped topping evenly over the surface of the completely cooled banana cake, slice into squares, and serve immediately or store chilled.

title: PRIME RIB WITH HORSERADISH YOGURT SAUCE (ON SIDE)      (GF/DF)

ingredients.name: bone-in or boneless prime rib roast (approx. 4 to 5 lbs)
plain unsweetened dairy-free yogurt (such as almond milk or coconut milk yogurt)
prepared horseradish (ensure it is certified gluten-free with no wheat-based thickeners)

steps: Tempering the meat: Remove the prime rib roast from the refrigerator 1 to 2 hours before cooking. Allowing the meat to come up closer to room temperature ensures it roasts perfectly evenly from the outer edges to the center.Season and crust: Preheat your oven to 450°F (230°C).
Pat the roast completely dry with paper towels. Rub all sides with the 2 tbsp of olive oil.

Mix the minced garlic, chopped rosemary, thyme, coarse salt, and black pepper in a small bowl, then press the herb mixture firmly into the meat to form a thick crust.High-heat sear: Place the seasoned roast on a rack inside a large roasting pan with the fat-side facing up. Roast at 450°F (230°C) for exactly 20 minutes to deeply sear the meat and lock in the juices.Low-heat roast: Without removing the roast, immediately drop the oven temperature to 325°F (165°C).

Continue cooking for 1 hour to 1 hour and 15 minutes, checking with a meat thermometer until the thickest part of the center reaches 120°F (49°C) for medium-rare.Rest and carve: Transfer the prime rib roast to a cutting board, tent it loosely with aluminum foil, and let it rest undisturbed for 20 to 30 minutes. The internal temperature will rise to 130°F (54°C) as it rests.Blend sauce and serve: While the roast rests, vigorously whisk the dairy-free yogurt, prepared horseradish, lemon juice, garlic powder, salt, and fresh chives together in a medium bowl until silky smooth.
Slice the prime rib between the bones and serve immediately with the zippy horseradish yogurt sauce strictly on the side.

title: LEMON PISTACHIO LOAF
steps: Preheat and mix dry: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C) and grease a standard loaf pan. Whisk flour, baking powder, and salt together.
Cream and combine: Cream butter and sugar until fluffy. Beat in eggs, lemon juice, and zest. Alternately mix in dry ingredients and milk. Fold in chopped pistachios.
Bake loaf: Pour into pan and bake 40 to 45 minutes until a toothpick inserted comes out clean. Cool completely.
Glaze: Whisk powdered sugar and lemon juice; drizzle smoothly across the top of the cooled loaf.

title: California Brocolli Salad with Cranberry and Feta Dressing (GF)
steps: Whisk dressing: Combine olive oil, apple cider vinegar, mashed feta cheese, honey, salt, and pepper in a bowl; whisk until uniform.
Assemble bowl: Combine chopped broccoli florets, dried cranberries, and sunflower seeds in a large salad bowl.
Toss salad: Pour the feta dressing over the broccoli base and toss thoroughly until fully coated.
Chill and serve: Cover and refrigerate for 15 minutes to let the broccoli absorb the vinaigrette flavors before serving.

title: Chicken Vegetable in light Broth (GF/DF)
steps: Sauté veggies: Heat olive oil in a pot over medium heat. Sauté sliced carrots and celery for 5 minutes until softening.
Simmer broth: Pour in chicken broth, add diced zucchini, salt, and pepper. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer covered for 10 minutes.
Heat meat through: Add shredded cooked chicken to the clear broth and simmer uncovered for 3 minutes until hot; serve immediately.

title: Carved Pork Tenderloin with Brown Butter and Garlic Sauce (GF/DF)
steps: Sear pork: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Season pork with salt and pepper. Sear in a hot skillet with olive oil for 2 minutes per side.
Roast meat: Transfer skillet to oven and roast 15 to 20 minutes until internal temperature reads 145°F (63°C); rest 5 minutes, then carve.

Brown butter sauce: Melt dairy-free butter alternative in a pan over medium-low heat until it foams and turns golden. Add garlic slices for 1 minute, then deglaze with broth and lemon juice; simmer 2 minutes.
Serve: Arrange carved pork slices on a platter and spoon the rich garlic brown butter sauce over top.

title: TURKEY BREAST WITH BREAD STUFFING (DF)
steps: Make stuffing: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Sauté onion and celery in dairy-free butter for 5 minutes. Toss with bread cubes, broth, and sage in a dish; bake covered for 20 minutes.
Season turkey: Rub turkey breast with olive oil, salt, pepper, and poultry seasoning.
Roast fully: Place turkey in a separate roasting pan and roast for 1 hour to 1 hour 15 minutes until internal temperature hits 165°F (74°C).
Serve together: Let turkey rest for 10 minutes, carve into thick slices, and serve hot alongside the baked bread stuffing.

title: COCONUT SHRIMP WITH LIME DIPPING

ingredients.name: large shrimp (peeled, deveined, tails left on)
juice and zest of 1 fresh lime

steps: Set up station: Place flour in first shallow bowl. Whisk eggs in second bowl. Combine shredded coconut and Panko breadcrumbs in third bowl.
Dredge and coat: Dip each shrimp into flour, shaking off excess, then dip into eggs, and press firmly into coconut-panko mixture to coat fully.
Shallow fry: Heat ½ inch of oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until it reaches 350°F (175°C). Fry shrimp in batches for 2 minutes per side until golden brown and crispy.
Serve with dip: Drain on paper towels and serve immediately with a quick dip made by stirring lime juice and zest into sweet chili sauce.

title: VEAL SHANK WITH SPICED AVOCADO LIME SAUCE
steps: Preheat and sear: Preheat oven to 325°F (160°C). Season veal shanks heavily with salt and pepper. Heat olive oil in a Dutch oven; sear shanks for 4 minutes per side until deeply browned; remove.
Sauté and deglaze: Cook diced onion in the same pot for 3 minutes. Pour in white wine, scraping the bottom, and simmer 3 minutes. Add beef broth.
Braised tender: Return veal shanks to the liquid, cover tightly with a lid, and braise in the oven for 2 hours until fork-tender.
Blend sauce and serve: Blend avocados, lime juice, jalapeño, cilantro, garlic, cumin, and salt until ultra-smooth. Slice veal and serve hot with a pool of the cold, creamy avocado sauce.

title: Slow roasted Flank Roast with Red Wine Sauce (GF/DF)

ingredients.name: flank steak (rolled and tied tightly with butcher twine)

steps: Preheat and season: Preheat oven to 325°F (160°C). Rub the tied flank roast with olive oil, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper, pressing spices into meat.

Sear and roast: Heat an oven-safe skillet over high heat. Sear the roast for 2 minutes per side to form a deep crust. Transfer the skillet directly to the oven and roast for 1 hour until internal temperature reads 135°F (57°C) for medium-rare; remove to rest.

Reduce wine sauce: Place skillet back on stove over medium heat (handle is hot). Melt 1 tbsp butter and sauté diced onion for 3 minutes. Pour in red wine and beef broth, adding the rosemary sprig. Boil hard for 8 minutes until reduced by half.
Finish and carve: Turn off heat, remove rosemary, and whisk in cold butter for a glossy finish. Remove twine, slice flank roast across the grain, and spoon sauce over top.

title: SHRIMP CURRY (MADRAS)

ingredients.name: brown sugar Finish: Juice of 1/2 lime

steps: Sauté aromatics: Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Cook the chopped onion for 5 minutes until soft, then stir in minced garlic and grated ginger for 1 minute until fragrant.
Bloom spices: Add Madras curry powder and turmeric to the pan. Stir constantly for 1 to 2 minutes to toast the spices and release their aromatic oils.

Simmer base: Pour in coconut milk, broth, fish sauce, and brown sugar. Stir well to dissolve the spices. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer uncovered for 5 minutes to thicken slightly.

Cook shrimp and serve: Add the shrimp to the simmering sauce. Cook for 3 to 4 minutes until they turn pink and opaque. Turn off heat, stir in fresh lime juice, garnish with chopped cilantro, and serve hot.

title: GRILLED TILAPIA PERUVIAN GREEN SAUCE
steps: Blend green sauce: Place cilantro, jalapeños, garlic, mayonnaise, grated cheese, lime juice, salt, and 1 tbsp olive oil into a blender. Blend on high speed for 1 to 2 minutes until perfectly smooth. Pour into a bowl, cover, and chill.
Preheat grill: Preheat your outdoor grill or indoor grill pan to medium-high heat (375°F to 400°F). Clean grates thoroughly and oil them well to prevent sticking.

Season fillets: Pat tilapia fillets completely dry with paper towels. Brush both sides evenly with 2 tbsp olive oil. Mix garlic powder, onion powder, cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper together, then rub evenly over both sides of the fish.

Grill and serve: Place seasoned tilapia directly onto the hot grates. Cook undisturbed for 4 to 5 minutes until it releases easily. Flip carefully and grill for another 2 to 3 minutes until opaque and flaky. Serve hot with a generous drizzle of the chilled green sauce.

title: TURKEY CUTLET WITH ARUGULA&PARM
steps: Bread the cutlets: Set up three shallow bowls with flour, beaten eggs, and Panko breadcrumbs. Season turkey cutlets with salt and pepper. Dip each cutlet into flour, then egg, and press firmly into the Panko until fully coated.

Shallow fry: Heat 3 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Fry the breaded turkey cutlets for 3 to 4 minutes per side until the crust is deeply golden brown and the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C). Remove to paper towels to drain.

Toss salad topping: While cutlets drain, whisk 2 tbsp olive oil, lemon juice, salt, and pepper together in a medium bowl. Toss in the fresh baby arugula and halved cherry tomatoes until lightly coated.

Assemble plate: Place a warm, crispy turkey cutlet onto each serving plate. Pile a generous handful of the dressed arugula and tomato salad directly on top of each cutlet, and garnish heavily with shaved Parmesan cheese.

title: ROASTED EGGPLANT WITH TAHINI

ingredients.name: toasted pine nuts or pomegranate seeds

steps: Preheat and prep: Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C) and line a large baking sheet with parchment paper.
Season eggplant: Place eggplant wedges into a large bowl. Drizzle generously with olive oil, and toss with salt and black pepper until the flesh completely absorbs the oil.

Roast tender: Arrange the wedges in a single flat layer on the baking sheet, leaving space between them. Roast for 20 to 25 minutes, flipping halfway through, until the skins are wrinkled and the flesh is deeply browned and completely tender.

Whisk dressing and serve: Whisk tahini paste, warm water, lemon juice, grated garlic, and salt together in a small bowl until it forms a smooth, pourable cream sauce. Arrange warm eggplant on a platter, drizzle dressing generously over top, and garnish with parsley and pine nuts.

title: SEARED CATFISH LEMON BEURRE BLANC SAUCE

ingredients.name: (1 stick) cold unsalted butter (cut into 1/2-inch cubes)

steps: Dust fish: Pat catfish fillets completely dry. Mix flour, paprika, a pinch of salt, and black pepper in a flat dish. Coat fillets lightly, shaking off excess.
Sear catfish: Heat olive oil and 1 tbsp butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Fry catfish for 3 to 4 minutes per side until golden brown, crispy, and opaque; remove to a warm plate.

Start wine reduction: Pour white wine, lemon juice, and minced shallot into the same skillet over medium heat, scraping up pan bits. Simmer for 3 to 4 minutes until the liquid reduces to about 1 tablespoon.

Whisk butter sauce: Turn heat down to ultra-low. Add the cold butter cubes one single cube at a time, whisking constantly. Wait until each cube is completely melted and emulsified before adding the next. Once a thick, creamy sauce forms, remove from heat, strain out shallots if desired, pour over seared catfish, and top with chives.

title: CLASSIC TREACLE TART (BUTTER TART PIE)

ingredients.name: unbaked 9-inch pie crust (blind-baked and cooled completely)
golden syrup (Lyle's preferred, or light molasses)
fine fresh breadcrumbs (from white sandwich bread)
zest and juice of 1 large lemon
large egg (beaten with 1 tbsp water) Serve: Clotted cream or vanilla ice cream

steps: Warm syrup: Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C). Gently warm the golden syrup in a medium saucepan over low heat just until fluid and runny (do not boil).
Incorporate breadcrumbs: Remove saucepan from heat. Stir in the fresh fine white breadcrumbs, fresh lemon juice, and grated lemon zest until fully combined and the crumbs absorb the syrup evenly.
Fill shell: Pour the warm syrup-crumb mixture smoothly into the prepared, blind-baked pie crust, flattening the surface with the back of a spoon.

Bake till set: Brush the exposed pastry crust edges evenly with egg wash. Bake uncovered for 40 to 45 minutes until the pastry is golden-brown and the sweet filling is set and crusted over. Cool completely on a wire rack to solidify before slicing.

title: DOVER SOLE WITH CREAMY PARSLEY SAUCE AND HERB OIL

ingredients.name: Dover sole fillets (approx. 5 to 6 oz each, skinless, patted dry)

steps: Make herb oil: Blanch parsley and chives in boiling water for 10 seconds, then shock in ice water. Squeeze out all moisture. Blend herbs, olive oil, lemon juice, and salt on high for 1 minute until vibrant green; strain through a fine sieve.

Simmer cream sauce: Melt 1 tbsp butter in a small pan over medium heat. Sauté minced shallot for 2 minutes. Pour in broth and heavy cream; simmer gently for 5 minutes until thickened. Stir in chopped parsley, salt, and white pepper; keep warm.

Dredge and sear fish: Season dry sole fillets with salt and pepper, then dust lightly in flour, shaking off excess. Heat olive oil and 2 tbsp butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Sear fillets for 2 to 3 minutes per side until golden and opaque.
Plate: Spoon a warm pool of creamy parsley sauce onto each serving plate, place a sole fillet carefully on top, and drizzle artfully with the vibrant green herb oil.

title: GRILLED SHRIMPS WITH MANGO SALSA
steps: Mix salsa: Combine diced mango, red bell pepper, red onion, chopped cilantro, lime juice, and salt in a medium bowl. Stir thoroughly, cover, and let sit at room temperature.
Season shrimp: Thread shrimp onto soaked wooden or metal skewers. Brush all sides with olive oil, then season evenly with chili powder, garlic powder, and salt.
Grill hot: Preheat your outdoor grill or indoor grill pan to high heat. Oil the grates well. Place shrimp skewers down and grill undisturbed for 2 to 3 minutes until charred and pink.
Flip and serve: Flip skewers and grill for 1 to 2 more minutes until fully opaque. Remove shrimp from skewers, pile onto a platter, and smother generously with the fresh mango salsa.

title: SPINACH STRAWBERRY SALAD ALMOND & GOAT CHEESE HONEY MUSTARD
steps: Whisk dressing: Combine extra-virgin olive oil, apple cider vinegar, honey, Dijon mustard, salt, and pepper in a small jar; shake vigorously until emulsified.
Assemble bowl: Place fresh baby spinach and sliced strawberries into a large salad bowl; toss gently.
Garnish layers: Top greens with crumbled goat cheese and toasted sliced almonds.
Dress and serve: Drizzle the honey mustard vinaigrette over the salad right before serving and toss lightly.

title: PAN SEARED SALMON WITH PINEAPPLE SALSA (GF/DF)
steps: Toss salsa: Combine diced pineapple, red bell pepper, red onion, chopped cilantro, lime juice, and salt in a bowl. Stir well, cover, and set aside.
Season fillets: Pat salmon fillets completely dry with paper towels. Rub the flesh side with olive oil, then season evenly with smoked paprika, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper.

Pan-sear skin down: Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Place fillets skin-side down, pressing down gently for 10 seconds. Cook undisturbed for 4 to 5 minutes until the skin is perfectly crisp.
Flip and finish: Flip fillets to the flesh side and cook for another 2 to 3 minutes until medium-cooked. Transfer to serving plates and smother generously with the cold pineapple salsa.

title: STUFFED BAKED POTATO
steps: Bake potatoes: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Pierce clean potatoes with a fork, rub skins with oil and salt, and bake 50 minutes until soft.
Open core: Slice a line down the center of each hot potato and squeeze ends to pop open; fluff interior flesh with a fork.
Stuff cavities: Mash 1 tbsp butter and 2 tbsp sour cream inside each potato. Top heavily with shredded Cheddar cheese and crumbled bacon bits.
Melt cheese: Return to the oven for 3 minutes until cheese melts completely; garnish with sliced green onions and serve hot.

title: CHOCOLATE RASPBERRY PAVLOVA
steps: Prep oven: Preheat oven to 300°F (150°C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper, drawing an 8-inch circle on it.
Whip meringue: Beat egg whites to soft peaks. Gradually add superfine sugar 1 tablespoon at a time on high speed until stiff, glossy peaks form. Fold in cornstarch, vinegar, and sifted cocoa powder.

Shape meringue: Pile meringue onto the parchment circle, smoothing edges to form a mound with a slight dip in the center. Lower oven to 250°F (120°C) and bake 1 hour 15 minutes; turn off oven and let cool inside completely.
Top cake: Whip heavy cream and powdered sugar. Pile whipped cream into the center of the pavlova base and scatter fresh raspberries over top.

title: ROASTED VEGETABLE AND FALAFEL ON BREAD WITH YOGURT SAUCE
steps: Whisk yogurt sauce: Stir Greek yogurt, minced garlic, lemon juice, chopped fresh dill, and a pinch of salt together in a bowl until smooth.
Warm bases: Toss flatbreads or pita breads into a dry skillet or oven for 1 minute to soften and warm through.
Layer roasted veg: Place warm flatbreads down, distribute roasted red pepper strips and roasted zucchini slices evenly across centers.
Assemble platter: Place 3 warm falafel balls onto each flatbread, drizzle heavily with the cold garlic yogurt sauce, fold over, and serve hot.

title: SPAGHETTI ALLA NORMA
steps: Crisp eggplant: Heat 3 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Sauté eggplant cubes with salt for 8 to 10 minutes until golden and soft; remove and set aside.
Simmer marinara: Add remaining 1 tbsp oil and sliced garlic to the skillet, cooking 1 minute. Pour in crushed tomatoes, oregano, and red pepper flakes; simmer uncovered on medium-low for 15 minutes.
Boil pasta: Cook spaghetti in boiling salted water until al dente; drain, reserving ¼ cup pasta water.
Combine and garnish: Toss spaghetti, cooked eggplant, and marinara together, splashing in pasta water if dry. Top generously with fresh basil and grated Ricotta Salata.

title: SAUTEED SPINACH WITH CARROT/MUSHROOM/ONION

ingredients.name: fresh baby spinach leaves (washed and thoroughly dried)

steps: Sauté hardy veggies: Heat olive oil and melt butter in a large skillet over medium heat. Sauté sliced onion and julienned carrots for 4 minutes until they begin to soften.
Brown mushrooms: Toss in the sliced mushrooms and minced garlic, sautéing for an additional 3 to 4 minutes until mushrooms are browned and onions are translucent.
Wilt greens: Turn heat to medium-high and add fresh baby spinach in large handfuls. Toss continuously with tongs for 2 minutes until leaves wilt.
Season: Remove from heat immediately, toss with kosher salt and black pepper, and serve hot.

title: CLASSIC BEEF CHILI WITH GARLIC BREAD
steps: Brown chili beef: Brown ground beef, diced onion, and green bell pepper in a large pot over medium-high heat for 6 minutes; drain excess fat.
Simmer chili sauce: Stir in chili powder, cumin, garlic powder, tomato sauce, diced tomatoes, and drained kidney beans. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 30 minutes.
Bake garlic bread: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Mix softened butter, minced garlic, and parsley. Spread evenly over the split Italian bread faces and bake 8 to 10 minutes until crispy.
Serve bowl: Ladle thick, hot beef chili into deep soup bowls and serve alongside hot slices of crispy garlic bread.

title: Prosciutto Wrapped Pears with Arugula and Blue Cheese (GF)

ingredients.name: firm but ripe pears (cut into 8 wedges each)

steps: Prep fruit: Wash and dry pears, cut into uniform wedges, and core them. Leave the skin on for stability and color contrast.
Wrap wedges: Place a few clean arugula leaves and a small piece of crumbled blue cheese against a pear wedge, then wrap a piece of prosciutto snugly around the center to secure everything.
Arrange plate: Place the wrapped pear elegant appetizer pieces on a large serving platter.
Finish presentation: Drizzle olive oil and balsamic glaze over the top in a zig-zag pattern, crack fresh black pepper over everything, and serve immediately.

title: Thai Fish Cakes with Cilantro Lime Dip

ingredients.name: firm white fish like cod or haddock (cut into chunks)
flat green beans (finely sliced into rings)

steps: Process fish base: Place fish chunks, egg, red curry paste, fish sauce, and sugar in a food processor. Pulse until it forms a smooth, sticky paste.
Fold mix-ins: Transfer paste to a bowl. Fold in sliced green onions and finely sliced green bean rings until evenly distributed.
Shape and pan-fry: Shape mixture into small, flat patties (about 2 inches wide). Heat vegetable oil in a skillet over medium heat. Fry cakes for 3 to 4 minutes per side until golden brown and firm.
Serve warm: Whisk sweet chili sauce, lime juice, and chopped cilantro together. Serve hot fish cakes alongside the sweet cilantro dip.

title: Roasted Beef Rump with Carmelized Onion and Apple Sauce (GF/DF)

ingredients.name: beef top sirloin or tenderloin roast

steps: Season roast: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Pat beef dry, rub with olive oil, and season with salt and pepper. Sear in a hot skillet for 2 minutes per side to form a crust.
Roast beef: Transfer meat skillet to the oven. Roast for 20 to 25 minutes until the internal temperature reaches 135°F (57°C) for medium-rare; let rest on a cutting board tented with foil.
Caramelize sauce: While beef cooks, melt dairy-free butter in a pan over medium heat. Sauté sliced onions and diced apples for 12 to 15 minutes until tender and deeply caramelized.
Deglaze pan: Stir in thyme, beef broth, and apple cider vinegar, scraping up pan bits. Simmer for 3 minutes until thick. Slice roast beef thinly and smother with the warm apple-onion sauce.

title: Lemon Curd Tart with Egg-white Meringue

ingredients.name: store-bought 9-inch unbaked pie crust (or homemade shortcrust pastry)

steps: Cook lemon curd: Whisk egg yolks, sugar, lemon zest, and lemon juice together in a saucepan over medium-low heat. Cook for 6 to 8 minutes, whisking constantly, until thick enough to coat a spoon. Remove from heat and stir in cold butter cubes until completely melted.
Fill tart shell: Pour the smooth lemon curd into the baked tart shell, smoothing the surface with a spatula; place in the refrigerator for 15 minutes.
Make glossy finish: Whip egg whites and cream of tartar in a clean bowl with an electric mixer until soft peaks form. Gradually beat in powdered sugar until stiff, glossy peaks form.
Broil or torch: Spread or pipe egg white peaks over the lemon curd. Place under an oven broiler for 1 to 2 minutes (or use a kitchen torch) until the tips turn golden brown; serve chilled.

title: Antipasti Skewers with Cured Meats and Italian Vegetables with Garlic Bread Spear
steps: Thread ingredients: Carefully thread a cherry tomato, a folded slice of meat, an artichoke heart quarter, a cheese cube, and a Kalamata olive onto each wooden skewer.
Arrange platter: Place the completed antipasti skewers in a neat overlapping circle pattern on a large serving platter.
Whisk dressing: Whisk together the extra-virgin olive oil, red wine vinegar, dried Italian seasoning, and a pinch of salt in a small bowl.
Drizzle and serve: Drizzle the herb dressing evenly across the skewers just before serving at room temperature.

title: Steamed Cous Cous with Herbs and Golden Raisins
steps: Boil broth base: Combine vegetable or chicken broth, olive oil or butter, and kosher salt in a small saucepan; bring to a rolling boil over high heat.
Steam grain: Remove saucepan from heat entirely. Pour in the dry couscous, stir once quickly to submerge, and immediately cover tightly with a lid.
Trap steam: Let the couscous sit undisturbed for 5 minutes off the heat to allow the grains to fully absorb the hot liquid.
Fluff and herb: Remove the lid and fluff the couscous grains thoroughly with a fork. Fold in the fresh chopped parsley, chives, and lemon juice before serving warm.

title: Sopa Ajoblanco (Spanish White Gazpacho) (GF)

ingredients.name: day-old white bread (crusts removed, soaked in water)

steps: Process paste: Place almonds, garlic cloves, and salt into a high-speed blender. Blend until a fine powdery crumb texture forms.
Blend base: Squeeze excess water from the soaked bread. Add bread to blender along with vinegar and 1 cup of the ice-cold water; blend until a thick paste sets.
Emulsify oil: Turn blender on low, slowly drizzle in the extra-virgin olive oil to emulsify. Pour in remaining cold water and blend on high until silky smooth.
Chill and serve: Transfer soup into a glass pitcher, cover, and chill for 2 hours. Serve cold in shallow bowls topped with halved green grapes.

title: Beef Strips with Sauteed Mushrooms and Pan Jus (GF/DF)

ingredients.name: sirloin or flank steak (sliced across grain into 1/2-inch strips)
mixed wild mushrooms (shiitake, cremini, oyster, sliced)

steps: Sear beef: Pat meat dry, season with garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Heat olive oil in a skillet over high heat. Sear beef strips for 2 minutes until browned; remove to a plate.
Sauté mushrooms: Lower skillet heat to medium, melt 1 tbsp butter, and sauté wild mushrooms and chopped shallot for 5 to 6 minutes until mushrooms are browned.
Build gravy: Add minced garlic and cook 30 seconds. Pour beef broth into pan, scraping bits. Simmer 3 minutes until broth reduces slightly.
Glaze meat: Stir in the remaining 1 tbsp cold butter and fresh parsley to gloss the sauce. Return beef strips to the skillet, toss for 1 minute to coat, and serve hot.

title: Roasted Turkey with Poultry Herb Sauce (GF/DF)

ingredients.name: turkey pan drippings plus chicken broth

steps: Preheat and rub: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Pat turkey breast dry. Rub with olive oil and coat evenly with poultry seasoning, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Roast juicy: Roast turkey breast in a pan for 1 hour to 1 hour and 15 minutes until a thermometer inserted in the thickest part reads 165°F (74°C); let rest 15 minutes.
Simmer drippings: Pour pan drippings into a measuring cup, skim off fat, add chicken broth to make 2 cups total, and pour into a saucepan over medium heat.
Thicken gravy: Whisk in the cornstarch slurry. Bring to a boil, stirring constantly, for 2 to 3 minutes until thickened smoothly; carve turkey and serve with gravy.

title: Shrimp Tempura with Sweet Dipping Sauce (GF/DF)

ingredients.name: large shrimp (peeled, deveined, tails left on, bellies scored to stay straight)

steps: Simmer dip sauce: Combine dashi, soy sauce, mirin, and grated ginger in a small saucepan. Simmer for 2 minutes to blend flavors; set aside.
Whisk cold batter: Whisk egg yolk and ice-cold sparkling water in a bowl. Add flour, cornstarch, and baking powder, mixing briefly with chopsticks—leave lumps in batter.
Dip and fry: Heat frying oil to 350°F (175°C) in a deep pot. Dust straight shrimp with extra flour, dip fully into the cold batter, and drop into hot oil.
Drain crisp: Fry 3 to 4 shrimp at a time for 2 to 3 minutes until light golden and crisp. Drain on a wire rack and serve hot with the dipping sauce.

title: Paprika and Honey Steamed Corn Kernels

ingredients.name: fresh or frozen sweet corn kernels (no need to thaw)
fresh chives or flat-leaf parsley (finely chopped)

steps: Melt the butter: Melt the unsalted butter in a large skillet over medium heat until it is hot, foaming, and completely fluid.Sauté the corn: Dump the sweet corn kernels directly into the hot buttered skillet. Increase the heat slightly to medium-high.

Sauté for 3 to 4 minutes, stirring frequently, until the corn is plump, tender, heated through, and turns a vibrant golden yellow.Season the kernels: Sprinkle the kosher salt, garlic powder, and freshly cracked black pepper evenly over the sautéed corn. Toss or stir vigorously for 1 minute so the spices fully dissolve into the warm butter glaze.Garnish and serve: Remove the skillet from the heat source.
Fold in the finely chopped fresh chives or parsley, transfer to a warm side dish, and serve immediately.

title: Poached Salmon with Cucumber and Herb Relish (GF/DF)

ingredients.name: low-sodium vegetable or chicken broth (or water)
English cucumber (peeled, seeded, and finely grated)

steps: Make the herbed sauce: Place the grated cucumber into a clean kitchen towel or cheesecloth and squeeze firmly over the sink to wring out all excess water. Transfer the dry cucumber to a medium mixing bowl.

Add the Greek yogurt, fresh dill, chives, lemon juice, minced garlic, salt, and white pepper. Whisk thoroughly until smooth and uniform, then cover and chill in the refrigerator.Build the aromatic bath: Combine the broth, optional white wine, sliced onion, lemon wheels, parsley sprigs, bay leaf, and salt in a large, wide skillet or shallow saucepan.

Bring the liquid to a boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to medium-low. Let it simmer uncovered for 5 minutes so the liquids absorb the aromatic flavors.Poach the salmon: Pat the salmon fillets completely dry with a paper towel.
Lower the fillets gently into the simmering liquid in a single layer—the liquid should just barely submerge the fish. Cover the skillet tightly with a lid and simmer gently for 8 to 10 minutes.

Do not let the liquid boil; a gentle bubble ensures the salmon remains incredibly tender and moist.Plate and garnish: The salmon is perfectly cooked when it turns opaque and flakes easily with a fork in the center. Use a wide slotted spatula to carefully lift each fillet out of the liquid, letting any excess broth drain off.
Place the salmon onto serving plates, top each fillet generously with a cold dollop of the cucumber-herb sauce, and serve warm or at room temperature.

title: Baked Chicken Thigh with Lemon and Herb Sauce (GF/DF)
steps: Preheat and prep: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Stuff chicken cavity with onion, lemon halves, and thyme sprigs.
Season and roast: Rub skin with olive oil, salt, and pepper. Roast breast-side up for 45 to 50 minutes until internal temperature hits 165°F (74°C).
Simmer jus: Remove chicken to a board to rest. Place roasting pan over stove burners on medium heat, pour in broth, and scrape up browned bits. Simmer 5 minutes, whisk in butter, and strain.
Serve: Carve chicken and spoon hot thyme-lemon jus over top.

title: Vegetarian stuffed Koosa (Riced Stuffed Zucchini) (GF/DF)

ingredients.name: medium green koosa (grey zucchini or marrow squash, cored)

steps: Mix filling: Stir the rinsed rice, diced tomato, parsley, mint, onion, olive oil, salt, and allspice together in a bowl until fully mixed.
Stuff koosa: Gently pack the rice mixture into the hollowed koosa, leaving a ½-inch gap at the top to allow the rice to expand.
Arrange and liquid: Place stuffed koosa snugly in a large pot. Whisk tomato sauce, vegetable broth, and smashed garlic together, then pour over the squash.
Simmer tender: Bring to a boil, then cover with a lid and reduce heat to medium-low. Simmer gently for 35 minutes until the rice inside is fully cooked and koosa is tender.

title: Baked Farro Pilaf with aromatic vegetables

ingredients.name: fresh thyme leaves (or 1/2 tsp dried thyme)

steps: Preheat and sauté: Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C). Heat the olive oil and butter in a medium oven-safe pan or Dutch oven over medium heat.

Add the diced onion and sliced mushrooms, sautéing for 5 to 6 minutes until the vegetables are soft and the mushrooms are browned.Toast the farro: Stir in the minced garlic and fresh thyme, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant. Add the rinsed farro to the pan and stir constantly for 2 minutes to lightly toast the grains and coat them in the oils.Simmer and cover: Pour in the broth, salt, and black pepper, stirring to scrape up any browned bits from the bottom.

Bring the liquid to a boil, then immediately turn off the heat and cover the pan tightly with a lid or a double layer of aluminum foil.Bake and fluff: Transfer the covered pan to the oven and bake for 30 to 35 minutes until the farro is tender and all the liquid is absorbed. Remove from the oven, let sit covered for 5 minutes, then fluff with a fork and fold in the Parmesan cheese and fresh parsley before serving.

title: Mexican Gazpacho Cold Soup (GF/DF)
steps: Chop the vegetables: Roughly chop the Roma tomatoes, peeled English cucumber, red bell pepper, red onion, and seeded jalapeño pepper. Mince the garlic cloves.Blend until smooth: Place all the chopped vegetables and minced garlic into a high-speed blender.

Add the extra-virgin olive oil, fresh lime juice, apple cider vinegar, ground cumin, kosher salt, and black pepper. Blend on high speed for 1 to 2 minutes until completely smooth, uniform, and slightly frothy.Chill thoroughly: Pour the gazpacho into a large glass pitcher or an airtight container.

Cover tightly and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, or until thoroughly chilled. This allows the fresh Mexican-inspired flavors and spices to mature and marry.Garnish and serve: Give the chilled soup a vigorous stir.
Ladle the cold soup evenly into four chilled bowls. Top each serving generously with a mountain of fresh diced avocado and chopped cilantro before serving cold.

title: Lemond Chiffon Cake with Icing

ingredients.name: cake flour (do not use regular all-purpose flour, as cake flour makes it extra soft)
granulated white sugar (divided into 1 cup and 1/2 cup)
finely grated lemon zest (only the yellow skin, not the bitter white part)
pure vanilla extractThe Meringue (Egg Whites): 8 large egg whites (at room temperature)
cream of tartar (this is a powder that helps stabilize the egg whites so they do not collapse)

steps: Separate the Eggs and Prep the PanEgg Temperature: Separate your eggs while they are cold straight from the fridge—the yolks are firmer and less likely to break. Place the 7 yolks in one large bowl and the 8 whites in a separate, completely clean metal or glass bowl. Let them sit on the counter for 30 minutes to reach room temperature.The Pan Rule: Preheat your oven to 325°F (160°C). Get a standard 10-inch two-piece Tube Pan (an angel food cake pan with a removable bottom and a funnel in the middle).
Important: Do not grease, butter, or flour the pan. Chiffon cake needs to literally "climb" up the dry walls of the pan to rise properly.2.

Mix the Main Batter BaseSift Dry Ingredients: In a huge mixing bowl, whisk together the cake flour, 1 cup of the granulated sugar, the baking powder, and the salt. Whisking thoroughly ensures the baking powder is perfectly distributed so the cake rises evenly.Add Liquids: Make a well (a hole) in the center of the flour mixture. Pour in the 7 egg yolks, vegetable oil, cold water, fresh lemon juice, 2 tablespoons of lemon zest, and vanilla extract.Whisk Smooth: Use a wire whisk to mix everything together vigorously for about 1 minute until the batter is completely smooth, glossy, and free of flour lumps. Set this bowl aside.3. Whip the Egg Whites (The Secret to Chiffon)Clean Equipment: Ensure your mixer beaters and the bowl holding the 8 egg whites are 100% clean and free of oil, grease, or leftover egg yolk. A single drop of fat will prevent the whites from whipping.Start Frothy: Using an electric hand mixer or stand mixer fitted with the whisk attachment, beat the egg whites on medium speed until they look frothy like soap bubbles (about 1 minute). Add the cream of tartar.Add Sugar Slowly: Turn the mixer speed up to high. While the mixer is running, slowly sprinkle in the remaining ½ cup of granulated sugar, one tablespoon at a time. Do not dump it all in at once.Watch for Stiff Peaks: Continue beating for 4 to 5 minutes. Stop the mixer and lift the beaters straight up. The egg whites should form a glossy, thick, marshmallow-like foam, and the tips of the peaks should stand straight up without bending over. This is called "stiff peaks."4. Fold the Batter GentlyThe 1/3 Rule: Pour about one-third of your whipped egg whites into the large bowl with the yellow flour batter. Use a rubber spatula to stir it in normally. This lightens up the heavy batter so the rest of the whites don't pop.The Folding Motion: Add another third of the egg whites. This time, do not stir. Instead, cut the spatula straight down through the center of the bowl, sweep it along the bottom, and flip the batter over itself. Turn the bowl slightly and repeat. This motion is called "folding" and keeps the trapped air in the egg whites from escaping.Final Fold: Add the final third of the whites and fold gently just until you no longer see any white streaks.5.

Bake and Cool Upside DownBake: Gently pour the fluffy batter into your ungreased tube pan. Run a butter knife through the batter in a circle pattern to pop any large hidden air pockets. Bake on the middle oven rack for 45 to 50 minutes. The top should be springy to the touch and a toothpick inserted into the center should come out completely clean.The Gravity Cool: Immediately remove the cake from the oven. Do not remove the cake from the pan. Turn the entire pan upside down. If your pan has little metal legs on the rim, let it rest on those. If it doesn't, balance the center tube of the pan over the neck of a glass bottle or funnel. Let the cake hang upside down for 1.5 to 2 hours until completely cold. Hanging prevents the delicate chiffon cake from collapsing under its own weight while cooling.Release: Once cold, turn the pan right-side up. Run a long, thin knife firmly against the outer plastic or metal wall of the pan in a steady circle to release the cake. Lift the center tube out, then slide the knife along the bottom plate to release the base. Place the cake on a serving platter.6.

Glaze the CakeWhisk Glaze: Whisk the powdered sugar, melted butter, lemon juice, and 1 teaspoon of lemon zest together in a bowl until smooth. It should be thick but pourable like warm glue. If it's too thick, add a few extra drops of lemon juice.Pour and Set: Pour the icing over the top crown of the cake, letting it drip decoratively down the sides. Let the icing sit for 15 minutes to form a light crust before slicing with a serrated bread knife.

title: Brined Pork Tenderloin with Feta and Olive Salsa (GF)

ingredients.name: large pork tenderloin (approx. 1 to 1.5 lbs, silver skin trimmed)

steps: Brine the pork: Whisk the cold water, kosher salt, and brown sugar together in a large bowl or zip-top bag until the salt and sugar fully dissolve. Drop in the smashed garlic cloves, rosemary sprig, and the trimmed pork tenderloin.

Seal tightly and place in the refrigerator to brine for 4 hours. This steps forces moisture into the lean meat so it stays incredibly juicy during cooking.Prep the salsa: While the pork completes its brine, combine the chopped Kalamata olives, crumbled feta cheese, diced tomato, minced red onion, and fresh chopped parsley in a medium bowl.

Drizzle with 1 tablespoon of extra virgin olive oil and the lemon juice. Stir the mixture gently so the feta doesn't mash, cover, and set aside at room temperature.Season and sear: Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C).
Remove the pork tenderloin from the brine and discard the liquid. Thoroughly pat the meat completely dry with paper towels—dry meat sears better.

Rub the tenderloin with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, then season all sides evenly with the garlic powder and black pepper. (You do not need extra salt, as the brine has already deeply seasoned the meat).

Heat an oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat and sear the pork for 2 minutes per side until a golden-brown crust forms.Roast, rest, and serve: Transfer the skillet with the seared pork directly into the preheated oven. Roast uncovered for 15 to 20 minutes.

Check the internal temperature with a meat thermometer; remove the pork from the oven as soon as the thickest part reads 145°F (63°C). Place the pork on a cutting board, tent loosely with foil, and let it rest for 10 minutes to lock in the juices.
Slice into thick medallions, arrange on a platter, and smother generously with the room-temperature feta and olive salsa.

title: Spicy Peanut Soba Noodle Salad (DF)

ingredients.name: to 2 tsp sriracha or chili garlic sauce (adjust to taste)

steps: Boil the soba noodles: Bring a large pot of water to a rolling boil over high heat (do not salt the water, as soba noodles typically contain salt). Drop in the soba noodles and cook for 5 to 7 minutes (check package instructions), stirring occasionally to prevent clumping.Rinse and chill: Drain the noodles immediately in a colander.

Rinse them thoroughly under cold running water for 30 seconds while gently tossing them with your hands to remove excess surface starch and stop the cooking process. Drain completely.Whisk the peanut dressing: Whisk the smooth peanut butter, soy sauce, toasted sesame oil, rice vinegar, honey, grated garlic, grated ginger, and sriracha together in a medium bowl until a thick paste forms.

Whisk in warm water, one tablespoon at a time, until the dressing thins out into a smooth, pourable consistency.Toss and serve: Place the cold, dry soba noodles into a large mixing bowl. Add the shredded carrots, shredded red cabbage, julienned cucumber, and sliced red bell pepper.

Pour the spicy peanut dressing over the top and toss thoroughly using tongs until everything is evenly coated. Transfer to a serving platter, garnish generously with chopped roasted peanuts and sliced green onions, and serve with lime wedges.

title: Wild Rice with Apricot and Dried Cranberry

ingredients.name: uncooked 100% wild rice (thoroughly rinsed in a fine-mesh strainer)

steps: Rinse and sauté: Place the wild rice into a fine-mesh strainer and rinse thoroughly under cold water. In a medium saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat.

Sauté the diced onion for 4 to 5 minutes until soft and translucent, then add the minced garlic and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.Simmer the rice: Add the rinsed wild rice, broth, salt, and black pepper to the saucepan. Bring the liquid to a rolling boil over high heat, then immediately reduce the heat to low.

Cover tightly with a lid and let simmer undisturbed for 40 to 45 minutes until the grains burst open to reveal their fluffy interiors.Steam and plump: Remove the saucepan from the heat source but leave the lid securely on. Let the rice steam undisturbed for 5 minutes.

Remove the lid and drain away any small amount of excess unabsorbed liquid if any remains at the bottom of the pan.Fold in sweet elements: While the rice is still hot, gently fold in the chopped dried apricots, dried cranberries, fresh thyme leaves, and toasted sliced almonds using a fork. The residual steam will plump up the dried fruit beautifully.
Transfer to a serving dish and serve warm or at room temperature.

title: Cheesy Chili White Bean Dip with Pita Chips

ingredients.name: large pita breads (cut into 6 triangles each)
(15 oz each) Cannellini or Great Northern beans (drained and rinsed)
prepared classic beef or vegetarian chili (warm)
sharp Cheddar or Monterey Jack cheese (shredded)

steps: Bake the pita chips: Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C). Arrange the pita triangles in a single flat layer across two large baking sheets.
Drizzle them evenly with 3 tablespoons of olive oil, then dust with the chili powder, garlic powder, and salt. Bake for 6 to 8 minutes until completely crisp and golden brown.

Set aside to cool.Process the bean base: Place the drained white beans, extra virgin olive oil, lime juice, garlic cloves, ground cumin, salt, and black pepper into a food processor or high-speed blender. Blend on high speed for 1 to 2 minutes, scraping down the sides as needed, until the beans transform into a completely smooth, velvety purée.Layer the dip: Spread the smooth white bean dip evenly across the bottom of an 8-inch cast-iron skillet or a shallow oven-safe baking dish.

Spoon the warm prepared chili directly over the center of the bean layer, spreading it slightly but leaving a small border of the white beans visible. Smother the entire top surface with the shredded cheese and scatter the jalapeño rounds over the cheese.Melt and serve: Place the skillet into the oven at 400°F (200°C) for 6 to 8 minutes, or turn on the oven broiler for 2 to 3 minutes, until the cheese layer is completely melted, bubbling, and turns lightly golden-brown.
Garnish immediately with the fresh chopped cilantro and serve hot alongside your crispy chili-dusted pita chips.

title: HONEY LEMON GARLIC CHICKEN BREAST

ingredients.name: skinless chicken breasts (pounded to an even 1/2-inch thickness)
fresh squeezed lemon juice (approx. 1 large lemon)
red pepper flakes (optional)Finish: Fresh parsley (chopped) and extra lemon wedges

steps: Marinate the chicken: Whisk together the olive oil, fresh lemon juice, honey, minced garlic, lemon zest, dried herbs, salt, black pepper, and optional red pepper flakes in a small bowl until the honey completely dissolves. Place the chicken breasts into a shallow dish or zip-top bag and pour the marinade over them.

Turn to coat, seal, and refrigerate for 30 minutes.Preheat the pan: Heat a large skillet (preferably cast iron or a heavy-bottomed pan) over medium heat. Add a tiny splash of oil to coat the bottom.Sear the chicken: Remove the chicken from the marinade, letting the excess drip off, but do not discard the remaining liquid.
Place the chicken breasts directly into the hot skillet. Cook undisturbed for 5 to 6 minutes.
Keep a close eye on the heat, as the sugar in the honey can cause the chicken to char quickly.Glaze and rest: Flip the chicken breasts over. Pour the remaining marinade directly into the skillet.

Cook for another 5 to 6 minutes, constantly spooning the bubbling pan juices over the chicken until the liquid thickens into a glossy, sticky glaze and the internal temperature of the chicken reaches 165°F (74°C). Transfer the chicken to a platter, pour the glaze over top, and let rest for 5 minutes before slicing.
Garnish with fresh parsley.

title: BBQ Pork Hush Puppies with Garlic Dip

ingredients.name: vegetable or peanut oil (for deep frying)

steps: Whisk the garlic dip: Combine the mayonnaise, minced garlic, fresh lemon juice, salt, and black pepper in a small serving bowl. Whisk thoroughly until smooth and creamy.

Cover and place in the refrigerator to let the flavors blend while you prepare the hush puppies.Prepare the batter: Whisk the yellow cornmeal, all-purpose flour, baking powder, sugar, salt, garlic powder, and cayenne pepper together in a large bowl. In a separate medium bowl, stir together the beaten egg, buttermilk, and melted butter.

Fold the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients gently with a rubber spatula just until combined.Fold in the pork: Add the finely chopped pulled pork and minced sweet onion into the thick batter. Fold gently until they are evenly distributed throughout the mixture.

Let the completed batter rest at room temperature for 10 minutes to thicken up.Fry and serve: Heat 2 inches of vegetable oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat until it reaches a steady 365°F (185°C). Drop rounded tablespoons of the batter into the hot oil using a small cookie scoop, frying 6 to 8 at a time.
Fry for 3 to 4 minutes per batch, rolling them occasionally, until deeply golden brown and cooked through. Drain on a paper towel-lined plate and serve hot with the garlic dip.

title: Creamy Mac’N’Cheese Bake
steps: Boil pasta and preheat: Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C) and lightly grease a 9x13-inch baking dish with butter. Bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil.
Cook the macaroni for 2 minutes less than the package directions suggest so it stays firm al dente. Drain and set aside.Build the cream sauce: Melt ¼ cup of butter in a large pot over medium heat.
Whisk in the flour and cook constantly for 1 to 2 minutes until golden. Slowly pour in the warm milk and heavy cream, whisking vigorously to avoid lumps.
Whisk in the salt, garlic powder, ground mustard, black pepper, and cayenne. Simmer for 3 to 4 minutes until thickened.Melt cheese and toss: Turn off the heat.

Add 2 cups of the shredded cheddar and all of the Gruyère cheese one handful at a time, stirring until completely melted and smooth. Fold the cooked macaroni into the cheese sauce until every noodle is fully coated.Assemble and bake: Pour the macaroni mixture into the prepared baking dish.
Sprinkle the remaining 1 cup of shredded cheddar cheese evenly over the top. Mix the Panko breadcrumbs, melted butter, and fresh parsley in a small bowl, then scatter them over the cheese layer.
Bake uncovered for 20 to 25 minutes until the sauce is bubbling at the edges and the breadcrumbs are deeply golden brown. Let rest for 10 minutes before serving.

title: Chicken Marsala with Creamy Mushroom Sauce (DF)

ingredients.name: skinless chicken breasts (pounded to an even 1/2-inch thickness)
gluten-free flour blend (or white rice flour, for dredging)
unsweetened cashew cream (or full-fat canned coconut milk, shaken well)

steps: Dredge the chicken: Mix the gluten-free flour, salt, and black pepper together in a shallow dish. Pat the pounded chicken breasts completely dry with paper towels, then coat each piece lightly in the flour mixture, thoroughly shaking off any excess.Sear the cutlets: Heat 1 tbsp of olive oil and 1 tbsp of dairy-free butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat.

Add the chicken breasts and sear for 3 to 4 minutes per side until they turn a beautiful golden brown and are cooked through. Remove the chicken to a warm plate and cover with foil.Sauté the mushrooms: Add the remaining 1 tbsp of olive oil and 1 tbsp of dairy-free butter to the same skillet over medium heat.
Toss in the sliced mushrooms and chopped shallot. Sauté for 5 to 6 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the mushrooms release their liquid and become deeply browned.

Stir in the minced garlic and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.Build the creamy sauce: Pour the Marsala wine and chicken broth into the skillet, using a wooden spoon to scrape up all the flavorful browned bits stuck to the bottom. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to medium and let simmer for 5 minutes until the liquid reduces by half.

Stir in the dairy-free cashew cream (or coconut milk) and lemon juice, simmering for another 2 minutes until the sauce thickens slightly into a rich velvety glaze. Nestled the warm chicken and any accumulated juices back into the skillet, spooning the sauce over the meat.
Garnish with fresh parsley and serve hot.

title: Pudding Chomeur with Vanilla Ice Cream

ingredients.name: pure maple syrup (Grade A amber preferred)

steps: Preheat and prep: Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C) and lightly grease an 8x11-inch or 9-inch square baking dish with butter. Whisk the all-purpose flour, baking powder, and salt together in a medium bowl to remove lumps.Cream and mix batter: Beat the room-temperature butter and granulated sugar together in a large bowl with an electric mixer for 2 to 3 minutes until light and fluffy.
Add the egg and vanilla extract, beating until completely smooth. Alternately add the dry flour mixture and the whole milk in batches, stirring gently with a spatula just until a thick batter forms.

Spread the batter evenly into the bottom of the prepared dish.Boil the sauce: Combine the pure maple syrup, brown sugar, heavy cream, butter, and a pinch of salt in a medium saucepan over medium heat. Bring the mixture to a boil, stirring constantly to dissolve the sugar, and let it boil for exactly 1 minute.Pour and bake: Carefully pour the boiling hot maple sauce directly over the raw cake batter in the baking dish—do not stir them together.

The batter will rise through the hot liquid as it bakes. Place the dish into the preheated oven and bake for 30 to 35 minutes until the cake top is deeply golden brown, a toothpick inserted into the cake center comes out clean, and the maple sauce is bubbling thickly underneath.
Let cool for 15 minutes to allow the sauce to set, then serve warm.

title: Buttermilk Fried Chicken (***BONE-IN***) (DF)

ingredients.name: skin-on chicken pieces (drumsticks, thighs, and breasts cut in half)
peanut oil or vegetable oil (for deep frying)

steps: Marinate the chicken: Whisk together the buttermilk, hot sauce, salt, garlic powder, onion powder, and smoked paprika in a large bowl. Submerge the chicken pieces fully in the liquid.

Cover the bowl and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, or overnight for the best results.Prepare the flour dredge: Whisk the all-purpose flour, cornstarch, garlic powder, onion powder, smoked paprika, cayenne, salt, and black pepper together in a large, shallow baking dish. Drizzle 3 tablespoons of the buttermilk marinade from the chicken bowl into the flour and mix with a fork to create small clumps for extra-crispy bits.Dredge the chicken: Remove each chicken piece from the buttermilk, letting the excess drip off, and drop it into the flour mixture.

Press the flour firmly onto all sides of the chicken to ensure full coverage. Transfer the coated pieces to a wire rack and let them rest for 15 minutes so the coating sets.Fry until golden: Heat 2 inches of oil in a large cast-iron skillet or Dutch oven over medium-high heat until it reaches 350°F (175°C).

Carefully lower 3 to 4 pieces of chicken into the oil, frying in batches to avoid crowding. Fry dark meat for 12 to 14 minutes and white meat for 8 to 10 minutes, flipping halfway through, until the crust is golden brown and the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C).
Drain on a clean wire rack and serve hot.

title: Indian Pudding – Cornmeal and Molasses Cake with Vanilla Ice Cream
steps: Scald milk and cook meal: Pour 3 cups of the whole milk into a heavy-bottomed saucepan over medium heat. Bring it to a bare simmer until tiny bubbles form around the edges.

Slowly whisk in the yellow cornmeal in a steady stream to prevent lumps. Reduce the heat to low and cook for 15 minutes, stirring constantly with a wooden spoon, until the mixture thickens to the consistency of porridge.Incorporate flavors and fats: Remove the saucepan from the heat.

Stir in the dark molasses, unsalted butter, granulated sugar, ground cinnamon, ground ginger, ground nutmeg, and salt until the butter is completely melted and the mixture is uniform.Temper and fold eggs: Preheat your oven to 325°F (165°C) and grease a 1½ or 2-quart baking dish. Whisk a ladleful of the hot cornmeal mixture into the beaten eggs to warm them gently.

Pour the egg mixture back into the saucepan and stir vigorously until fully incorporated.Pour and bake: Pour the batter into the prepared baking dish. Carefully pour the remaining 1 cup of cold whole milk directly over the top of the batter—do not stir it in, as this creates the signature soft, porridge-cake texture with a custard-like center.
Bake uncovered for 2 hours until the edges are set and the top is deeply browned. Let cool for 20 minutes and serve warm topped with a large scoop of vanilla ice cream.

title: Grilled Clams with Cucumber and Scallion Salad (GF/DF)

ingredients.name: fresh littleneck clams (scrubbed thoroughly and rinsed)

steps: Clean clams: Soak clams in cold salted water for 15 minutes to purge sand, then scrub shells clean and discard any open ones that don't close when tapped.
Preheat grill: Preheat your outdoor grill to high heat (approx. 450°F / 230°C).

Grill shells: Place clams directly onto the clean grill grates. Close the lid and cook for 5 to 8 minutes. Watch closely and remove individual clams with tongs as soon as their shells pop open wide; discard any that stay closed.

Toss and serve: Pile open hot clams into a large serving bowl. Pour melted butter, olive oil, lime juice, grated garlic, finely diced cucumber, and sliced green onions over top. Toss quickly and serve immediately.

title: Muffuletta Olive Salad on Grilled Crostini (DF)

ingredients.name: pitted green Sicilian or Castelvetrano olives (chopped)
giardiniera (Italian pickled vegetables, drained and chopped)

steps: Chop ingredients: Coarsely chop the green olives, Kalamata olives, giardiniera pickled vegetables, and drained capers.
Combine base: Toss all the chopped olives and pickled vegetables together in a medium-sized glass bowl.
Whisk vinaigrette: Whisk the extra virgin olive oil, red wine vinegar, minced garlic, dried oregano, and black pepper together in a small cup.
Marinate and serve: Pour dressing over olive mix and stir thoroughly. Cover and let marinate at room temperature for at least 15 minutes (or overnight) before serving as a relish spread.

title: Penne Pasta with Chicken and Sausage in Aglio Olio (Garlic & Olive Oil) Sauce

ingredients.name: skinless chicken breasts (cut into bite-sized strips)
Italian sausage links (mild or hot, casings removed and crumbled)
crushed red pepper flakes (optional, for heat)
kosher salt (plus more for boiling water)
grated Pecorino Romano or Parmesan cheese

steps: Boil the pasta: Bring a large pot of heavily salted water to a rolling boil over high heat. Drop in the penne pasta and cook according to the package directions until it reaches a firm al dente consistency.

Crucial Step: Before draining the pasta, carefully scoop out and reserve ½ cup of the starchy, cloudy pasta cooking water. Drain the penne and set aside.Sear the proteins: While the pasta cooks, heat 2 tablespoons of regular olive oil in a large, deep skillet over medium-high heat.

Add the crumbled sausage meat and sauté for 4 to 5 minutes, breaking it apart with a wooden spoon, until it is beautifully browned. Toss in the chicken strips, season with a pinch of salt and pepper, and sauté everything together for an additional 5 minutes until the chicken is fully cooked through and opaque.

Transfer the cooked meat and all pan juices to a clean plate and cover with foil.Build the garlic oil (Aglio Olio): Wipe out the skillet and return it to the stovetop over medium-low heat. Pour in the ⅓ cup of extra virgin olive oil.
Add the thinly sliced garlic and the optional red pepper flakes. Cook very gently for 2 to 3 minutes, stirring constantly.

Watch the pan like a hawk—the garlic should slowly soften and turn a delicate pale golden color. If it turns dark brown, it will taste bitter, and you will need to restart the sauce.Emulsify and toss: Dump the hot drained penne pasta, the seared chicken, the sausage, and their resting juices directly into the garlic oil skillet.

Pour in ¼ cup of your reserved warm pasta water and increase the heat to medium. Toss vigorously for 1 to 2 minutes; the starchy water and olive oil will fuse together into a glossy, light sauce that clings to the penne.
Turn off the heat, fold in the fresh chopped parsley and the grated Pecorino Romano cheese, and serve immediately.

title: Summer Rice Wraps with Peanut Dipping Sauce (GF/DF)

ingredients.name: cooked small shrimp or shredded chicken

steps: Soften wrapper: Pour warm water into a wide, shallow dish. Dip one rice paper sheet into the water for 5 seconds until pliable but still slightly firm; lay flat on a clean cutting board.
Layer fillings: Place a few mint leaves in the center, followed by a small handful of vermicelli noodles, julienned carrots, cucumbers, and a line of shrimp or chicken.
Roll tightly: Fold the bottom edge of the paper up over the fillings, fold in both sides snugly, and roll upward tightly to seal like a burrito.
Mix dip and serve: Whisk peanut butter, hoisin sauce, lime juice, and warm water together until smooth. Serve the fresh summer rolls immediately alongside the dipping sauce.

title: Baked Salmon Medallion with Black Bean and Sweet Pepper Salsa (GF/DF)

ingredients.name: skinless salmon medallions (approx. 6 oz each, cut thick and rounded)
sweet bell peppers (mixed red, yellow, and orange, finely diced)

steps: Preheat and prep salmon: Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C) and line a rimmed baking sheet with parchment paper. Mix the garlic powder, cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper together in a small bowl.

Pat the salmon medallions completely dry with a paper towel, brush them evenly with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, and press the spice mixture firmly onto all sides of the fish.Bake the medallions: Arrange the seasoned salmon medallions on the prepared baking sheet, leaving space between each piece. Bake uncovered for 10 to 12 minutes, depending on thickness, until the salmon turns opaque on the outside and flakes easily with a fork in the center.Toss the fresh salsa: While the salmon bakes, combine the rinsed black beans, finely diced sweet bell peppers, cucumber, minced red onion, and chopped fresh cilantro in a medium mixing bowl.

Whisk the 2 tablespoons of olive oil, fresh lime juice, orange juice, and salt together in a small cup, then pour over the salsa and toss thoroughly to coat.Plate and serve: Spoon a generous bed of the colorful black bean and sweet pepper salsa onto each serving plate. Carefully lift the hot baked salmon medallions using a wide spatula, place them directly over the cold salsa, and serve immediately.

title: Baked Cauliflower Steak with Cajun Corn Salsa (GF/DF)
steps: Cut the cauliflower steaks: Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C) and line a large rimmed baking sheet with parchment paper. Trim the outer green leaves off the cauliflower heads, keeping the central stems intact (the stem holds the steaks together).

Cut each head strictly in half down the exact center, then slice a 1-inch thick flat "steak" from each half. You will get 2 steaks per head (4 total); save the loose leftover florets for another meal.Season the steaks: Whisk the garlic powder, onion powder, smoked paprika, thyme, oregano, salt, cayenne pepper, and black pepper together in a small bowl.

Arrange the 4 cauliflower steaks on your prepared baking sheet. Brush both sides of the steaks generously with 2 tablespoons of olive oil, then coat them evenly with the Cajun seasoning blend, rubbing it firmly into the crevices.Bake until tender: Place the baking sheet into the preheated oven.

Bake uncovered for 20 to 25 minutes, carefully flipping the delicate steaks over halfway through the cooking time using a wide spatula, until the edges are beautifully charred and caramelized, and the central stems are completely fork-tender.Toss the salsa and serve: While the steaks bake, combine the corn kernels, finely diced red bell pepper, minced red onion, and chopped cilantro in a medium mixing bowl. Whisk the 2 tablespoons of olive oil, lime juice, and salt together, then pour over the salsa and stir well.
To serve, place a hot Cajun cauliflower steak onto each plate and smother it generously with a mountain of the cool corn salsa.

title: Watermelon and Feta Salad with Goddess Dressing (GF)
steps: Blend the dressing: Place the mayonnaise, sour cream, fresh parsley, chives, tarragon, lemon juice, garlic clove, kosher salt, and black pepper into a blender or food processor. Blend on high speed for 1 to 2 minutes until the herbs are completely puréed and the dressing transforms into a smooth, vibrant green cream.

Transfer to a jar and chill.Assemble the crisp base: Arrange the cold watermelon cubes, thin cucumber half-moons, and sliced red onion across a large, shallow serving platter or inside a wide salad bowl. Toss them very gently with your hands to distribute the colors without bruising the fruit.Dress the salad: Drizzle about half of the green goddess dressing evenly over the watermelon, cucumber, and onion base.

(Save the remaining dressing in the refrigerator for up to 5 days).Garnish and serve: Scatter the crumbled chilled feta cheese, torn fresh mint, and sliced basil leaves generously over the top layer. Finish with a final light turn of freshly cracked black pepper and serve immediately while cold.

title: Turkey Pot Roast with Savoury Gravy (GF/DF)

ingredients.name: boneless turkey thigh or breast roast

steps: Sear meat: Heat oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Season turkey roast with salt and pepper; sear 5 minutes until browned on all sides, then remove.
Sauté onions: Cook diced onion in the same pot fat for 3 minutes until translucent. Pour in broth, scraping up browned bits.
Simmer pot: Return turkey roast to the pot, add chopped carrots, quartered potatoes, and bay leaf. Bring to a boil, cover tightly, and reduce heat to low.
Cook tender: Simmer gently for 1.5 to 2 hours until the turkey is fork-tender. Slice meat and serve surrounded by the pot vegetables and juices.

title: Chicken Francese – Dusted Chicken Breast in Buttery Lemon Sauce (GF/DF)

ingredients.name: chicken breasts (pounded to a uniform 1/2-inch thickness)
gluten-free 1-to-1 baking flour (or white rice flour, for dusting)
water (Ensure all dredging elements are Certified Gluten-Free)
dry white wine (such as Pinot Grigio)

steps: Dredge and egg coat: Mix the gluten-free flour, kosher salt, and white pepper together in a shallow dish. Whisk the eggs and water together in a second shallow bowl.

Pat the chicken breasts completely dry with paper towels. Coat each piece lightly in the flour mixture, shaking off excess, and then submerge completely in the beaten egg mixture so it is fully coated in egg wash.Pan-fry golden: Heat the olive oil and 1 tablespoon of the dairy-free butter alternative together in a large skillet over medium-high heat until hot and foaming.

Lift the chicken from the egg wash, letting the excess drip off, and place directly into the skillet. Fry for 3 to 4 minutes per side until the egg coating is puffed, golden brown, and the chicken is cooked through.

Transfer the chicken to a warm plate and cover with foil.Simmer wine reduction: Pour the white wine, chicken broth, and fresh lemon juice directly into the hot skillet over medium heat. Use a wooden spoon to scrape up any savory browned bits stuck to the bottom of the pan.

Bring the liquid to a rapid boil and let it cook uncovered for 5 minutes until reduced by half. Drop the fresh lemon slices into the bubbling sauce for the final 2 minutes of simmering.Thicken glaze and serve: Turn the heat down to low.

Whisk in the remaining 1 tablespoon of dairy-free butter alternative and the chopped fresh parsley until the sauce becomes smooth, glossy, and thickens slightly. Return the cooked chicken breasts and their accumulated plate juices back into the skillet, spooning the velvety lemon sauce over the meat for 1 minute until glazed and hot.
Serve immediately.

title: Vegetable Enchiladas with Melted Cheese

ingredients.name: Monterey Jack or sharp Cheddar cheese (shredded, divided)

steps: Preheat and cook filling: Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C) and lightly grease a 9x13-inch baking dish. Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.

Sauté the diced zucchini, yellow bell pepper, and red onion for 5 to 6 minutes until they begin to soften.Add aromatics and beans: Stir in the minced garlic, chili powder, cumin, salt, and black pepper, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant. Remove the skillet from the heat source and fold in the black beans, corn kernels, and ½ cup of the shredded cheese until evenly distributed.Assemble the enchiladas: Pour ½ cup of the red enchilada sauce across the bottom of the prepared baking dish to create a non-stick layer.

Dip each warm tortilla lightly into the remaining sauce to coat, spoon a generous portion of the veggie filling down the center, and roll up tightly. Place the rolled enchiladas seam-side down in the baking dish.Bake and melt cheese: Pour the remaining enchilada sauce smoothly over the top of all the rolled tortillas, ensuring the edges are well coated so they don't dry out.

Sprinkle the remaining 1½ cups of shredded cheese evenly over the sauce. Bake uncovered for 20 to 25 minutes until the sauce is bubbling at the edges and the cheese layer is completely melted, gooey, and lightly golden-brown.
Garnish with chopped cilantro and sliced green onions before serving hot.
